**5.** When serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM on a defendant, the arresting officer must:

A) A) Have a supervisor present as witness
B) B) Activate the Body-Worn Camera
C) C) Obtain written consent from the ADA
D) D) Have the form notarized
Back:
**Answer: B**

The arresting officer must activate the BWC when serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM. (P.G. 218-48)

Front:
**6.** When marking firearm-related evidence, where should initials be scratched on a shell casing recovered from a crime scene?

A) A) The exterior of the casing
B) B) The bottom/base of the casing
C) C) The inside of the casing
D) D) Nowhere — shell casings are not individually marked
Back:
**Answer: C**

Shell casings are marked on the INSIDE. DO NOT mark the exterior or bottom. (P.G. 218-23)

Front:
**7.** Firearms requiring laboratory analysis must be delivered to the patrol borough office by what time daily?

A) A) 2400 hours
B) B) 0600 hours
C) C) 0100 hours
D) D) 1800 hours
Back:
**Answer: C**

All invoiced firearms requiring Firearms Analysis Section examination must be delivered by 0100 hours daily. Delay requires exigent circumstances AND permission from the precinct CO/duty captain. (P.G. 218-23)

Front:
**8.** A PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE being sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed how many line items?

A) A) 10
B) B) 15
C) C) 20
D) D) 25
Back:
**Answer: C**

INVOICES sent or potentially sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed twenty line items. (P.G. 218-01)

Front:
**9.** Which unit is the ONLY unit authorized to cancel alarms for firearms?

A) A) Auto Crime Unit
B) B) Recovering command desk officer
C) C) Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
D) D) Property Clerk Division
Back:
**Answer: C**

Cancellation of firearms alarms will be made ONLY by the Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. The recovering command will NOT cancel. (P.G. 218-23)

Front:
**10.** When seizing computer evidence, what should a member do if the computer needs to be powered off?

A) A) Press the on/off switch
B) B) Hold the power button for 10 seconds
C) C) Unplug the computer from the wall outlet
D) D) Close the laptop lid
Back:
**Answer: C**

Do NOT use the on/off switch. Unplug from the wall outlet only. Prior to removing the plug, photograph the monitor screen. (P.G. 218-31)

Front:
**11.** Invoiced property categorized as Investigatory (non-vehicle) will be disposed of by the Property Clerk after what period if no retention request is submitted?

A) A) 6 months
B) B) 1 year
C) C) 2 years
D) D) 15 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Property Clerk will dispose of investigatory property (non-vehicle) after one year from the date of invoice if no retention request is submitted. Vehicles/boats are disposed of after 15 days. (P.G. 218-55)

Front:
**12.** When processing non-evidence currency, the command messenger must be accompanied by whom when delivering the deposit bag to the bank?

A) A) A detective
B) B) The invoicing officer
C) C) Another uniformed member of the service or Evidence/Property Control Specialist
D) D) The desk officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

Currency is delivered to the bank's night deposit vault by the command messenger accompanied by another uniformed member or Evidence/Property Control Specialist. (P.G. 218-36)

Front:
**13.** Under P.G. 218-38, what is the minimum value of currency/negotiable instruments that requires notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit for forfeiture processing?

A) A) $500
B) B) $1,000
C) C) $5,000
D) D) $10,000
Back:
**Answer: B**

Seized U.S. currency and/or negotiable instruments valued at $1,000 or more that are deemed forfeitable require notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit (24/7). (P.G. 218-38)

Front:
**14.** When processing a deceased person's property, what must the patrol supervisor obtain before directing a body search?

A) A) Permission from the commanding officer
B) B) Conferral with the Medical Examiner
C) C) A search warrant
D) D) Written consent from next of kin
Back:
**Answer: B**

The patrol supervisor must confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner PRIOR to directing a member to search the body for valuables and identification documents. (P.G. 218-28)

Front:
**15.** What is the maximum number of fireworks bags that should be transported in an RMP?

A) A) 2 bags
B) B) 4 bags
C) C) 6 bags
D) D) 10 bags
Back:
**Answer: B**

As a general rule, no more than four bags of fireworks should be transported in an RMP, placed in the rear seat away from heat/spark sources. (P.G. 218-53)

Front:
**16.** A handgun license surrender investigation must be completed and forms forwarded to the License Division within how many days?

A) A) 10 days
B) B) 15 days
C) C) 30 days
D) D) 60 days
Back:
**Answer: C**

The investigation must be completed and all related forms forwarded to the License Division, through channels, within thirty days of date the HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE was received at command. (P.G. 218-45)

Front:
**17.** Under the vendor enforcement procedures, what is the supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement?

A) A) One supervisor for three officers
B) B) One supervisor for five officers
C) C) One supervisor for eight officers
D) D) One supervisor for ten officers
Back:
**Answer: B**

Supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement shall not exceed one supervisor for five police officers. (P.G. 218-39)

Front:
**18.** When the desk officer receives a request for the return of investigatory evidence, the release must be authorized or denied within how many days?

A) A) 5 days
B) B) 10 days
C) C) 15 days
D) D) 30 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

The desk officer must authorize release no more than ten days after receipt of the request, if property is no longer required as evidence. If denied, claimant may request CO/designee review within 5 days. (P.G. 218-02)

Front:
**19.** What is the removal fee when a licensed food vendor's vehicle, cart, or stand is removed by a Department vehicle?

A) A) $20.00
B) B) $25.00
C) C) $50.00
D) D) $65.00
Back:
**Answer: D**

The removal fee when property is removed by a Department vehicle is $65.00. If NOT removed by Department vehicle, the fee is $20.00. (P.G. 218-43)

Front:
**20.** When FORMS Property becomes inoperable for more than six hours and the problem is isolated to the invoicing command only, what should the desk officer instruct?

A) A) Use pre-printed serialized INVOICES
B) B) Contact the Property Clerk Division for manual processing
C) C) Remove property to an adjoining command and invoice there
D) D) Wait for the system to be restored
Back:
**Answer: C**

When the outage exceeds six hours and is isolated to the command, the invoicing member removes property to an adjoining command and invoices in compliance with current procedures. The invoicing command (where the incident occurred) is selected as the invoicing command in FORMS. (P.G. 218-57)

Front:
**21.** Under P.G. 218-04, a found revolver with no connection to an arrest or criminal investigation would be:

A) A) Delivered to the Police Laboratory for analysis
B) B) Delivered to the Firearms Analysis Section (FAS) for tracing
C) C) Delivered direct to the Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required)
D) D) Held at the command until a detective supervisor directs otherwise
Back:
**Answer: C**

Found revolvers not associated with an arrest or criminal investigation do NOT require laboratory analysis and go directly to the Property Clerk. By contrast, found firearms (semi-automatic handguns, etc.) DO go to the Lab. (P.G. 218-04)

Front:
**22.** Under P.G. 218-04, the Desk Officer must notify the Patrol Boro prior to what time that evidence is going to the Laboratory?

A) A) 0100 hours
B) B) 0030 hours
C) C) 2400 hours
D) D) 2200 hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Desk Officer notifies the Patrol Boro PRIOR to 0030 that there is evidence going to the Lab for delivery. The actual delivery must then occur on the 1st platoon PRIOR to 0100. If the desk officer fails to call the boro by 0030, the Borough Member/Clerical Officer will call command at 0100. (P.G. 218-04)

Front:
**23.** Under P.G. 218-04, which of the following forms is NOT attached to the Drugs transparent bag going to the Police Laboratory?

A) A) Property Clerk Invoice (PCI)
B) B) Request for Laboratory Examination
C) C) zFINEST printout
D) D) All of the above are NOT attached to the Drugs bag
Back:
**Answer: D**

The Drugs bag does NOT get a Request for Laboratory Examination, does NOT get a 61/Omni printout, and does NOT get a zFINEST printout. The ONLY paperwork attached to the Drugs bag is the PCI. (P.G. 218-04)

Front:
**24.** A member fires their service weapon during an officer-involved shooting. The recovered shell casings from the scene should be marked:

A) A) On the exterior of the casing
B) B) On the inside of the casing only
C) C) On the bottom of the casing
D) D) Not marked — shell casings from officer-involved shootings are never marked
Back:
**Answer: B**

Discharged shell casings are marked on the INSIDE only — never on the exterior or bottom. This applies to all shell casings regardless of the circumstances. (P.G. 218-23)

Front:
**25.** When a member wishes to have a firearm sent to the Lab but does NOT want it traced, what must they write and where?

A) A) "NO TRACE" in the remarks section of the INVOICE only
B) B) "DO NOT TRACE" in block letters in the remarks section of BOTH the PCI AND the Request for Lab Exam
C) C) "HOLD FOR TRACE" in the arrest paperwork
D) D) Nothing — tracing is automatic and cannot be declined
Back:
**Answer: B**

The phrase "DO NOT TRACE" must be written in block letters in the remarks section of both documents. If just one document is marked, the firearm may still be traced. (P.G. 218-23)

Front:
**26.** Officers recover an abandoned vehicle with no plates on a highway. It has been unattended for 7 hours. Under VTL 1224, how should this vehicle be classified?

A) A) Not abandoned — must be unattended 24 hours
B) B) Abandoned — unattended more than 6 hours with no plates
C) C) Derelict — subject to Sanitation disposal
D) D) Evidence — subject to Dept. Tow only
Back:
**Answer: B**

Under VTL 1224, a vehicle left unattended for more than 6 hours with no plates is deemed abandoned. This is the shortest of the four time thresholds. (P.G. 218-21)

Front:
**27.** A tow operator, after being told the type and condition of a vehicle, refuses to accept a Rotation Tow assignment. The Desk Officer should:

A) A) Call the next tow operator without any documentation
B) B) Prepare a Report of Violation (LD6) and forward to DCWP; then call next operator in sequence
C) C) Notify the commanding officer and await further instructions
D) D) Prepare a Complaint Report (61) and forward to DCWP
Back:
**Answer: B**

When a tow operator refuses AFTER being informed of vehicle type and condition, the Desk Officer prepares a Report of Violation (LD6) and forwards it to the Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP). The next tow operator in sequence is then notified. (P.G. 218-21)

Front:
**28.** A complaint is received that a Rotation Tow company stole items from a towed vehicle. The correct form and routing is:

A) A) LD6 (Report of Violation) forwarded to DCWP
B) B) Complaint Report (61) forwarded to DCWP (duplicate, informational only)
C) C) LD6 forwarded to Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) Complaint Report (61) forwarded directly to the tow company's license bureau
Back:
**Answer: B**

Theft and other unlawful acts by tow companies are recorded on the Complaint Report (61) and the duplicate is forwarded to DCWP for informational purposes only. Overcharges use LD6. Know the distinction. (P.G. 218-21)

Front:
**29.** A patrol supervisor responds to a scene where a person has died alone in their apartment. The supervisor wants to search the body. What must be done FIRST?

A) A) Request Operations to notify the Public Administrator
B) B) Confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner
C) C) Obtain a search warrant from the DA
D) D) Seal the door with a SEAL FOR DOOR OF DOA PREMISES
Back:
**Answer: B**

The patrol supervisor must confer with the ME PRIOR to directing any member to search the body. This is a mandatory first step. (P.G. 218-28)

Front:
**30.** When securing fireworks for transport in an RMP, members should:

A) A) Place bags in the trunk, sealed, with windows closed
B) B) Place up to 4 bags in the rear seat with all windows opened minimum 2 inches
C) C) Place bags in the front passenger seat with air conditioning running
D) D) Call Bomb Squad for escort before any transport of fireworks
Back:
**Answer: B**

Fireworks are transported in the REAR SEAT of the RMP (NOT the trunk). All windows must be opened a minimum of 2 inches. Maximum 4 bags per RMP. For a significant amount, call FDNY through 311 for escort. (P.G. 218-53)

Front:
**31.** A Sanitation Department worker places a tag on an abandoned-looking vehicle and notifies NYPD. This automatically qualifies the vehicle for Rotation Tow.

A) A) True — the DSNY notification triggers the Ro-Tow program
B) B) False — the UMOS must make the final determination by visual inspection
C) C) True — but only if the vehicle has been tagged for more than 24 hours
D) D) False — only the Desk Officer can authorize Ro-Tow after DSNY notification
Back:
**Answer: B**

A DSNY sticker or notification does NOT automatically make a vehicle eligible for Ro-Tow. The Desk Officer directs a UMOS to inspect the vehicle and decide if it qualifies based on criteria found on the rear inside cover of the Ro-Tow Log. (P.G. 218-21)

Front:
**1.** Under P.G. 219-08, tire chains should NOT be installed on Department vehicles until there is more than how many inches of snow?

A) A) 2 inches
B) B) 3 inches
C) C) 4 inches
D) D) 6 inches
Back:
**Answer: C**

Tire chains are NOT installed until there is more than 4 inches of snow. Additionally, tire chains are NOT installed on front-wheel drive vehicles. (P.G. 219-08)

Front:
**2.** Which color fuel card is designated for the CRALO?

A) A) Green
B) B) White
C) C) Yellow
D) D) Orange
Back:
**Answer: C**

Card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator. (P.G. 219-11)

Front:
**3.** How often must the Integrity Control Officer audit the Domain Awareness System (DAS) and how many members must be reviewed?

A) A) Weekly, 3 random members
B) B) Monthly, 5 random members
C) C) Quarterly, 10 random members
D) D) Semi-annually, 5 random members
Back:
**Answer: B**

The ICO conducts a monthly DAS audit reviewing 5 random members. (P.G. 219-14)

Front:
**4.** If a portable radio is lost and the member was NOT carrying it in an authorized carrying case, the finding will be:

A) A) No fault
B) B) Accidental loss
C) C) Negligence
D) D) Misconduct
Back:
**Answer: C**

If a portable radio is lost and was not in an authorized carrying case at the time, the member is deemed negligent. (P.G. 219-15)

Front:
**5.** Which of the following is NOT a required notification when a portable radio is lost or stolen?

A) A) Operations Unit
B) B) Electronics Section
C) C) Fleet Services Division
D) D) Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
Back:
**Answer: C**

The four required notifications are: Operations Unit, Electronics Section, Internal Affairs Bureau, and Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. Fleet Services Division is not notified for radio losses. (P.G. 219-15)

Front:
**6.** Weekly vehicle inspections are conducted on what day and after which platoon has been posted?

A) A) Saturday, after the first platoon
B) B) Sunday, after the second platoon
C) C) Monday, after the first platoon
D) D) Friday, after the third platoon
Back:
**Answer: B**

The lieutenant platoon commander/patrol supervisor inspects all Department vehicles each Sunday after the second platoon has been posted. (P.G. 219-02)

Front:
**7.** Regulated infectious waste from patrol kits is picked up by:

A) A) Department of Sanitation
B) B) Medical Division messenger
C) C) FDNY Hazmat
D) D) Quartermaster Section
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Medical Division messenger picks up regulated infectious waste from the 32-gallon biohazard receptacle at the command. (P.G. 219-17)

Front:
**8.** Before a law enforcement action is initiated based on a License Plate Reader (LPR) alarm, the operator must:

A) A) Notify the patrol supervisor
B) B) Contact the Real Time Crime Center
C) C) Verify the vehicle status through NYSPIN
D) D) Photograph the license plate
Back:
**Answer: C**

It is absolutely essential that the LPR operator verify the current status of a vehicle through NYSPIN prior to initiating any law enforcement action. The LPR is NOT connected to NYSPIN and activates on partial scan matches. (P.G. 219-31)

Front:
**9.** NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes are NOT valid for use on which type of transit?

A) A) Subway
B) B) Local bus
C) C) MTA express bus
D) D) Staten Island Railway
Back:
**Answer: C**

OMNY Police Passes are not valid for use on MTA express buses. Besides this exception, they have no electronic restrictions and may be used on subways and public buses. (P.G. 219-26)

Front:
**10.** A vehicle with how many red light camera violations within a twelve-month period will be ineligible for a Restricted Parking Permit?

A) A) 3 or more
B) B) 5 or more
C) C) 10 or more
D) D) 15 or more
Back:
**Answer: B**

Vehicles with 5 or more red light camera violations OR 15 or more school speed camera violations within a twelve-month period are ineligible. (P.G. 219-29)

Front:
**11.** Commands with a single fuel tank (heating and generator) must maintain the fuel level above what percentage?

A) A) 25%
B) B) 50%
C) C) 75%
D) D) 90%
Back:
**Answer: B**

Commands with a single fuel tank must maintain the level above 50%. Commands with two separate tanks are responsible only for the heating tank; Building Maintenance Section maintains the generator tank. (P.G. 219-28)

Front:
**12.** When a member's firearm is lost and disciplinary action is warranted, the commanding officer must consult with:

A) A) Internal Affairs Bureau
B) B) Legal Bureau
C) C) Department Advocate's Office
D) D) Chief of Department
Back:
**Answer: C**

When disciplinary action is warranted for loss of or failure to safeguard a firearm, a consultation with the Department Advocate's Office is required to determine appropriate disciplinary action. (P.G. 219-21)

Front:
**13.** A member's firearm, shield, or ID card is lost within Police Headquarters (5th Precinct). The investigation will be conducted by:

A) A) The 5th Precinct commanding officer
B) B) A captain or above from the reporting member's command
C) C) The Headquarters Security Unit
D) D) Internal Affairs Bureau
Back:
**Answer: B**

At Police Headquarters or Police Academy, the loss is reported to the Headquarters Security Unit (or Police Academy Integrity Unit), but the investigation is conducted by a captain or above from the reporting member's command. (P.G. 219-21)

Front:
**14.** Which of the following is a permitted use of a Department tow vehicle?

A) A) Towing a civilian vehicle to a repair shop
B) B) Towing an inoperable Department vehicle
C) C) Removing vehicles for alternate side parking
D) D) Towing abandoned civilian vehicles to the pound
Back:
**Answer: B**

Department Tow has limited authorized uses: inoperable Department vehicle, evidence vehicle, parade/emergency path clearance, assisting ESU, and lifting heavy articles. (P.G. 219-09)

Front:
**15.** How many business days prior to foreign travel must a member request ITB Information Security Desk permission to bring a Department mobile device?

A) A) 2 business days
B) B) 3 business days
C) C) 5 business days
D) D) 10 business days
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members must request permission from the ITB Information Security Desk at least 5 business days prior to taking a Department mobile digital device to a foreign country. (P.G. 219-32)

Front:
**16.** During the daily vehicle inspection, a patrol officer discovers the vehicle is in a sector that includes a body of water. Which life safety equipment is required?

A) A) Life ring with rope attached
B) B) Two vest-type life jackets
C) C) Fire extinguisher and life ring
D) D) Life ring and two vest-type life jackets
Back:
**Answer: B**

When an RMP is assigned to a sector with a body of water, two vest-type life jackets are required. A life ring with rope is required when the command itself borders or contains a body of water — this applies to all RMPs in that command, not just those in a specific sector. (P.G. 219-01)

Front:
**17.** During a start-of-tour vehicle inspection, an operator finds a serious deficiency. The Platoon Commander is not available. To whom should the Patrol Supervisor report the serious deficiency?

A) A) Desk Officer
B) B) Operations Coordinator
C) C) Commanding Officer
D) D) Borough Commander
Back:
**Answer: C**

In the absence of the Platoon Commander, the Patrol Supervisor reports serious vehicle deficiencies directly to the Commanding Officer — not to the Desk Officer. (P.G. 219-01)

Front:
**18.** The weekly vehicle inspection requires the Lt. Platoon Commander to check which item that is NOT included in the operator's daily start-of-tour inspection?

A) A) Tires and spare
B) B) E-Z Pass tag serial number
C) C) Visual Message Signs
D) D) Portable radio
Back:
**Answer: C**

Visual Message Signs are specifically checked during the weekly inspection (219-02) but are NOT mentioned as part of the operator's daily (start-of-tour) inspection (219-01). This distinction has appeared on prior exams. (P.G. 219-02)

Front:
**19.** A member of the service discovers that their computer password has been compromised. According to P.G. 219-14, who must be notified?

A) A) Commanding Officer only
B) B) Integrity Control Officer only
C) C) Desk Officer/designated supervisor OR ICO
D) D) Internal Affairs Bureau and the Desk Officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

If a password is compromised or misused, the member must notify the D.O./designated supervisor OR the ICO (comply with P.G. 207-21 regarding corruption). The procedure does not require notifying IAB directly. (P.G. 219-14)

Front:
**20.** A UMOS loses a Department-issued NYCT MetroCard (Police Pass) in the confines of their command. Who investigates?

A) A) ICO
B) B) Commanding Officer
C) C) Operations Coordinator
D) D) Desk Officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

Loss or theft of a MetroCard (Police Pass) is processed per P.G. 219-20, where the Operations Coordinator conducts the investigation. Note the contrast: a damaged MetroCard is investigated by the ICO per P.G. 219-26. This distinction was tested on the 2017 Lieutenant's exam. (P.G. 219-20, 219-26)

Front:
**21.** A UMOS loses their shield in Nassau County. Which Desk Officer must be notified first?

A) A) Desk Officer at the pct of occurrence
B) B) Desk Officer 50 Pct
C) C) Desk Officer 105 Pct
D) D) Operations Unit directly
Back:
**Answer: C**

Nassau and Suffolk County losses are reported to Desk Officer 105 Pct. Westchester, Putnam, Rockland, or Orange County losses go to Desk Officer 50 Pct. Losses outside those residence counties require notifying the Operations Unit directly. (P.G. 219-21)

Front:
**22.** A commanding officer's firearm is lost and disciplinary action may be warranted. Before initiating a Command Discipline, the CO of the MOS concerned must:

A) A) Notify IAB first and obtain a log number
B) B) Consult with the Department Advocate
C) C) Consult with the Legal Bureau
D) D) File a report with the Chief of Department
Back:
**Answer: B**

When a firearm is lost and discipline is contemplated, the CO of the MOS concerned MUST consult with the Department Advocate before initiating a CD or charges. This requirement applies specifically to firearms — not shields or ID cards. (P.G. 219-21)

Front:
**23.** A member's firearm is lost in the confines of Police Headquarters (5th Precinct). The 61 is recorded by the 5th Precinct. Who conducts the investigation?

A) A) The 5th Precinct Commanding Officer
B) B) The Headquarters Security Unit
C) C) A captain or above from the reporting member's command
D) D) Internal Affairs Bureau
Back:
**Answer: C**

When a loss occurs at Police Headquarters, the loss is reported to the HQ Security Unit (which records the 61), but the investigation is conducted by a captain or above from the reporting member's own command. A supervisor from HQ makes the notifications, and a captain or above from HQ investigates and prepares the reports. (P.G. 219-21)

Front:
**24.** When the Commanding Officer of a MOS distributes the Lost/Stolen Firearm/Shield/ID Card Report (Part D), the distribution instructions are found where on the form?

A) A) Top of the form
B) B) Front of the form
C) C) Bottom of the form
D) D) Rear of the form
Back:
**Answer: D**

The C.O. of the MOS distributes Part D per the REAR of the form. The C.O./Duty Captain distributes Part C per the BOTTOM of the form. These are different distribution instructions and this distinction has appeared on prior exams. (P.G. 219-21)

Front:
**25.** A CCRB requests information from a Department smartphone belonging to a member subject to a CCRB inquiry. The member notifies their ICO. The ICO then contacts which unit for guidance?

A) A) Internal Affairs Bureau
B) B) Legal Bureau
C) C) Operations Unit
D) D) Department Advocate's Office
Back:
**Answer: B**

When CCRB requests smartphone information: UMOS notifies ICO → ICO contacts Legal Bureau for guidance → IAB's CCRB Liaison then processes the request. The chain is UMOS → ICO → Legal Bureau → IAB handles the CCRB response. (P.G. 219-32)

Front:
**26.** An LPR device activates and shows a "hit" on a vehicle's license plate. Under what circumstance may the officer take law enforcement action WITHOUT first verifying through NYSPIN?

A) A) Never — NYSPIN verification is always required
B) B) When the LPR hot list was updated within the last 24 hours
C) C) When there are additional reasons for a lawful stop, such as an observed VTL infraction
D) D) Only when directed by a patrol supervisor
Back:
**Answer: C**

NYSPIN verification is "absolutely essential" UNLESS: (1) there are additional reasons for a lawful stop such as an observed VTL infraction, OR (2) circumstances provide sufficient reasonable suspicion to justify a Terry Stop/Level 3 encounter. In those cases, enforcement may proceed without the NYSPIN check. (P.G. 219-31)

Front:
**27.** An entry is made on a member's Force Record (10 card) after their firearm is reported lost. What date and time is used for the entry?

A) A) Date and time the firearm was actually lost
B) B) Date and time the member notified the Desk Officer
C) C) Date and time of the Command Log Entry
D) D) Date and time Part C of the Loss Form was completed
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Force Record (10 card) entry uses the date and time of the Command Log Entry — NOT the date and time of the actual loss. This is a specific and frequently tested distinction. (P.G. 219-21)

Front:
**1.** What is the #1 priority in CIMS incident management?

A) A) Site Management Operations
B) B) Investigative Operations
C) C) Life Safety Operations
D) D) Recovery/Restoration Operations
Back:
**Answer: C**

Life Safety is always the immediate #1 priority and supersedes all other operations. (P.G. 220-05)

Front:
**2.** What is the span of control ratio for law enforcement operations under CIMS?

A) A) 1:3 to 1:5
B) B) 1:3 to 1:7
C) C) 1:5 to 1:10
D) D) 1:8 to 1:10
Back:
**Answer: D**

The general span of control is 1:3 to 1:7, but law enforcement operations use a ratio of 1:8 to 1:10. (P.G. 220-05)

Front:
**3.** How many Incident Command Posts (ICPs) may be established per incident?

A) A) One per borough involved
B) B) One per agency
C) C) Only ONE (except nuclear incidents)
D) D) As many as needed based on incident size
Back:
**Answer: C**

There is only ONE ICP per incident. The exception is a nuclear weapon incident (anticipated, suspected, or actual), which uses borough-centric ICPs under Unified Command. (P.G. 220-19)

Front:
**4.** What is the composition of a Squad under CIMS?

A) A) 1 Sergeant + 5 Police Officers
B) B) 1 Sergeant + 8 Police Officers
C) C) 1 Lieutenant + 8 Police Officers
D) D) 2 Sergeants + 10 Police Officers
Back:
**Answer: B**

A Squad consists of 1 Sergeant and 8 Police Officers. (P.G. 220-25)

Front:
**5.** Who has authority to designate the Public Information Officer at an incident?

A) A) The Chief of Department
B) B) NYCEM
C) C) The Mayor's Press Office
D) D) The Operations Section chief
Back:
**Answer: C**

While the Incident Commander or Agency Incident Commanders may designate the PIO, the Mayor's Press Office has overriding authority and may designate the PIO. (P.G. 220-06)

Front:
**6.** Resources in "Available" status at a Staging Area must be ready for deployment within how many minutes?

A) A) 1 minute
B) B) 3 minutes
C) C) 5 minutes
D) D) 10 minutes
Back:
**Answer: B**

Resources at a Staging Area are in "Available" status and must be ready for deployment within 3 minutes. (P.G. 220-20)

Front:
**7.** Which agency is designated as the Coordinating Agency under CIMS?

A) A) NYPD
B) B) FDNY
C) C) NYC Emergency Management (NYCEM)
D) D) Mayor's Office of Operations
Back:
**Answer: C**

NYCEM is the designated Coordinating Agency. It facilitates, coordinates, and supports inter-agency activities but does NOT command the incident. (P.G. 220-15)

Front:
**8.** When a CBRN/HazMat incident involves actual or suspected crime or terrorism, what command structure is used?

A) A) Unified Command with NYPD, FDNY, DEP, and DOHMH
B) B) NYPD Single Command with an Incident Commander
C) C) FDNY Single Command
D) D) Unified Command with borough-centric ICPs
Back:
**Answer: B**

When a CBRN/HazMat incident involves actual or suspected crime or terrorism, NYPD exercises Single Command. (P.G. 220-02)

Front:
**9.** A ranking member arriving at an incident has how many options regarding the Command Element?

A) A) Two — assume command or make no change
B) B) Three — assume command, make no change, or leave the scene
C) C) Four — assume command (no CE), assume from current CE, make no change, designate a third party
D) D) Five — the four options above plus request a higher-ranking officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

The four Transfer of Command options are: (1) Assume command when no CE exists, (2) Assume command from current CE, (3) Make no change, (4) Designate a third party. (P.G. 220-17)

Front:
**10.** The Safety Officer may exercise emergency authority to directly stop unsafe activities under what condition?

A) A) When any safety concern is identified
B) B) When authorized by the Command Element
C) C) When life or safety of incident personnel is in imminent danger
D) D) Only at HazMat incidents
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Safety Officer may exercise emergency authority to directly prevent, stop, and/or correct hazardous activities when life or safety is in imminent danger — without waiting for chain of command approval. (P.G. 220-07)

Front:
**11.** Which General Staff section prepares the Incident Action Plan?

A) A) Operations Section
B) B) Logistics Section
C) C) Planning Section
D) D) Finance/Administration Section
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Planning Section chief is responsible for preparing and distributing the Incident Action Plan (IAP). (P.G. 220-10)

Front:
**12.** A formal written Incident Action Plan is required when an incident extends beyond what timeframe?

A) A) 12 hours
B) B) 18 hours
C) C) 24 hours
D) D) 48 hours
Back:
**Answer: C**

When an incident extends beyond 24 hours (multiple Operational Periods), a formal written IAP is required. (P.G. 220-28)

Front:
**13.** Who may activate a Citywide Incident Management Team (IMT)?

A) A) Any Deputy Chief or above
B) B) Chief of Patrol or Patrol Borough Commander
C) C) Police Commissioner, First Deputy Commissioner, or Chief of Department
D) D) Chief of Department or any Bureau Chief
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Citywide IMT is activated by the Police Commissioner, First Deputy Commissioner, or Chief of Department. The Borough IMT is activated by the Chief of Patrol or Patrol Borough Commander. (P.G. 220-28)

Front:
**14.** What is the correct CIMS incident sequence?

A) A) Life Safety → Investigation → Site Management → Recovery → Demobilization
B) B) Response → Life Safety → Site Management → Investigation → Recovery/Restoration → Demobilization
C) C) Response → Site Management → Life Safety → Investigation → Demobilization
D) D) Life Safety → Site Management → Recovery → Investigation → Demobilization
Back:
**Answer: B**

The correct CIMS incident sequence is: Initial Response, Life Safety Operations, Site Management Operations, Investigative Operations, Recovery/Restoration Operations, Demobilization. Phases may overlap with CE approval. (P.G. 220-18)

Front:
**15.** Which of the following is NOT a CIMS resource status condition?

A) A) Assigned
B) B) Available
C) C) Standby
D) D) Out-of-Service
Back:
**Answer: C**

The three CIMS resource statuses are Assigned (performing work tasks), Available (ready for assignment, deploy within 3 minutes), and Out-of-Service (not able to be assigned). "Standby" is not a CIMS status. (P.G. 220-25)

Front:
**16.** The Operations Section Post is generally:

A) A) Co-located with the ICP for efficiency
B) B) NOT co-located with the ICP
C) C) Located at the Staging Area
D) D) Located outside the Outer Perimeter
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Operations Section Post is generally NOT co-located with the ICP. The I/I Section Post is also NOT co-located with either the ICP or Operations Post. (P.G. 220-09, 220-19)

Front:
**17.** What form is used for Check-In recording at CIMS incidents?

A) A) ICS Form 201
B) B) Complaint Report
C) C) DETAIL ROSTER / ASSIGNMENT SHEETS (PD406-141)
D) D) Unusual Occurrence Report
Back:
**Answer: C**

The PD406-141 (DETAIL ROSTER / ASSIGNMENT SHEETS) or other appropriate forms are used for the Check-In process. (P.G. 220-24)

Front:
**18.** A Mobile Field Force consists of:

A) A) 1 to 3 Squads
B) B) 2 to 6 Squads
C) C) 4 to 8 Squads
D) D) 3 to 5 Squads
Back:
**Answer: B**

A Mobile Field Force consists of 2 to 6 Squads. Each Squad = 1 Sergeant + 8 Police Officers. (P.G. 220-25)

Front:
**19.** Under CIMS, the Incident Base and Camp are:

A) A) Required at every incident with more than 50 personnel
B) B) Activated only during Unified Command
C) C) NOT activated in NYC absent extraordinary circumstances
D) D) Managed by the Logistics Section only during planned events
Back:
**Answer: C**

Incident Base and Camp are NIMS ICS concepts that are NOT activated in NYC absent extraordinary circumstances. (P.G. 220-19)

Front:
**20.** What type of communications must be used for inter-agency communications at a CIMS incident?

A) A) Standard NYPD 10-codes
B) B) Agency-specific codes
C) C) Clear text with CIMS terminology
D) D) Encrypted digital communications only
Back:
**Answer: C**

All incident personnel must use clear text (no 10-codes) for inter-agency communications and use CIMS nomenclature/terminology. (P.G. 220-16)

Front:
**21.** Who has authority to designate the Liaison Officer under CIMS?

A) A) The Mayor's Press Office
B) B) NYCEM (NYC Emergency Management)
C) C) The Operations Section chief
D) D) The Planning Section chief
Back:
**Answer: B**

While the Incident Commander may designate the Liaison Officer, NYCEM has overriding authority and may designate the Liaison Officer. The Mayor's Press Office designates the PIO, not the Liaison Officer. (P.G. 220-08)

Front:
**22.** Resources must formally Check-In before reporting to which section for assignment?

A) A) Planning Section
B) B) Logistics Section
C) C) Operations Section
D) D) Finance/Administration Section
Back:
**Answer: C**

Resources must formally Check-In before reporting to the Operations Section for assignment (absent extraordinary circumstances). (P.G. 220-24)

Front:
**23.** The Safety Officer function is required by law at which type of incident?

A) A) Any incident with more than 20 personnel
B) B) Hazardous materials incidents
C) C) All Unified Command incidents
D) D) National Special Security Events
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Safety Officer function is required by law at hazardous materials incidents. (P.G. 220-07)

Front:
**24.** Under CIMS, Incident Objectives must be:

A) A) Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound
B) B) Attainable, Measurable, Adaptable/Flexible
C) C) Clear, Concise, Complete
D) D) Immediate, Actionable, Documented
Back:
**Answer: B**

Under Management by Objectives, CIMS Incident Objectives must be Attainable, Measurable, and Adaptable/Flexible. (P.G. 220-05)

Front:
**25.** Which of the following is an NYPD Single Command incident?

A) A) Building collapse
B) B) Structural fire
C) C) Hazardous materials release (no crime)
D) D) Hostage situation
Back:
**Answer: D**

NYPD Single Command incidents include: Civil Disturbance, EDP, Explosive Device/Bomb Threat, Hostage, Sniper, Special Event, Suspicious Package, and Water/Ice Rescue. Building collapse and structural fire are not NYPD Single Command; HazMat without crime is Unified Command. (P.G. 220-02)

Front:
**26.** Under a Unified Command, which of the following positions does each Agency Incident Commander pick independently (not jointly with other ICs)?

A) A) Planning Section Chief
B) B) Logistics Section Chief
C) C) Safety Officer
D) D) Liaison Officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

In Unified Command, each IC independently picks their own Safety Officer and their own Operations Section Chief. PIO, Liaison Officer, Planning Section Chief, Logistics Section Chief, Finance/Admin Section Chief, and Intelligence Section Chief are all jointly picked by the Co-ICs. (P.G. 220-05, 220-07)

Front:
**27.** At a CBRN/HazMat incident involving suspected terrorism, which agency serves as the primary Incident Commander?

A) A) FDNY
B) B) DEP
C) C) NYPD
D) D) NYCEM
Back:
**Answer: C**

When a CBRN/HazMat incident involves actual or suspected crime or terrorism, NYPD serves as the sole Incident Commander (Single Command). NYPD remains primary IC until it is determined there is no criminal or terrorist activity, at which point command reverts to Unified Command. (P.G. 220-02)

Front:
**28.** Which of the following incident types uses a Unified Command structure with FDNY, NYPD, PANYNJ, USCG, and NTSB as Potential Primary Agencies?

A) A) Civil Disturbance
B) B) Aviation Incident
C) C) Rail Incident
D) D) Explosion
Back:
**Answer: B**

Aviation Incidents use a Unified Command structure with FDNY and NYPD as Primary Agencies, and PANYNJ, USCG, and NTSB as Potential Primary Agencies/Subject Matter Experts. Rail Incidents use MTA, PANYNJ, NJT, Amtrak as PPAs. Explosions use DDC, DOB, HPD. (P.G. 220-02)

Front:
**29.** At a structural collapse incident, which agency is the Primary Agency?

A) A) NYPD
B) B) FDNY
C) C) DOB
D) D) DDC
Back:
**Answer: B**

Structural Collapse is an FDNY Single Command incident. This question has been tested on the Sergeant exam. Other FDNY Single Command incidents include: Confined Space Rescue, Elevator Incident, Entrapment/Impalement, and Fire. (P.G. 220-02)

Front:
**30.** The General Staff Section Chief who uses the keyword "MODULAR" to describe how the Incident Commander can staff the incident is associated with which concept?

A) A) The Incident Commander must use all five General Staff sections at every incident
B) B) The IC can run an operation with one or two people or any combination of staff positions
C) C) Only the Operations Section must always be activated
D) D) The Command Element always requires at least three Command Staff members
Back:
**Answer: B**

"MODULAR" is the keyword for how CIMS works — the structure is flexible and scalable. The IC can run an operation with one or two people or open any combination of staff positions as needed. No section is mandatory. (P.G. 220-01)

Front:
**31.** In a CBRN/HazMat incident that starts as NYPD Single Command, which agency is responsible for setting Hot, Warm, and Cold zones?

A) A) NYPD
B) B) FDNY
C) C) DEP
D) D) DOHMH
Back:
**Answer: C**

In Single-CBRN incidents, the Operations Section is Unified. DEP is responsible for Chemical — setting Hot, Warm, and Cold zones, and directing the mitigation effort. FDNY handles Life Safety, Search & Rescue, and Mass Decontamination. DOHMH handles Biological/Radiological zone-setting. NYPD handles overall site management and crime/terrorism assessment. (P.G. 220-02)

Front:
**32.** When the Finance/Administration Section Chief is not needed as a full General Staff section, which section may absorb its functions?

A) A) Operations Section
B) B) Planning Section
C) C) Logistics Section
D) D) Intelligence/Investigations Section
Back:
**Answer: B**

When minimal finance/admin tasks are required (e.g., only managing overtime), the Finance/Admin role may not be a full General Staff section. Instead, it could be one specialist person working for the Planning Section Chief (per PG 220-12, step 1a). (P.G. 220-12)

Front:
**33.** Under CIMS, which two General Staff sections can have Branches?

A) A) Operations and Planning
B) B) Operations and Intelligence/Investigations
C) C) Operations and Logistics
D) D) Planning and Finance/Administration
Back:
**Answer: C**

Operations and Logistics can have Branches. Operations can also have Groups/Sectors. Planning, Finance/Admin can have Units only. Intelligence/Investigations can have Groups/Sectors only. Of the sections that can have Units, only Logistics can also have Branches. (P.G. 220-01)

Front:
**34.** The Downed Tree incident type designates which agency as the Primary Agency?

A) A) NYPD
B) B) FDNY
C) C) DEP
D) D) Department of Parks & Recreation / FDNY
Back:
**Answer: D**

Downed Tree incidents are Primary Agency for Department of Parks & Recreation / FDNY. This is a Single Command incident that is often overlooked — it is not NYPD or FDNY alone. Correctional Facility Disturbance is DOC; Confined Space Rescue is FDNY. (P.G. 220-02)

Front:
**35.** Which agency has authority to designate the Liaison Officer in CIMS, and what is the history of that agency's name?

A) A) Mayor's Press Office, formerly known as the Mayor's Communications Office
B) B) NYCEM, formerly known as OEM (Office of Emergency Management)
C) C) DCPI, formerly known as the Press Secretary
D) D) DSNY, formerly known as the Sanitation Commission
Back:
**Answer: B**

NYCEM (NYC Emergency Management) has authority to designate the Liaison Officer. NYCEM was formerly known as OEM (Office of Emergency Management). Exam questions may use either name. The Mayor's Press Office designates the PIO, not the Liaison Officer. (P.G. 220-08)

Front:
**1.** Which of the following is a firearm prohibition under P.G. 221-01?

A) A) Discharging a firearm at a fleeing felon who presents an imminent threat
B) B) Firing warning shots
C) C) Discharging a firearm to protect from imminent physical injury
D) D) Discharging a firearm in self-defense
Back:
**Answer: B**

Warning shots are prohibited. Members SHALL NOT fire warning shots under any circumstances. (P.G. 221-01)

Front:
**2.** A CEW discharge in cartridge mode is classified as what level force incident?

A) A) Level 1
B) B) Level 2
C) C) Level 3
D) D) Level 4
Back:
**Answer: A**

CEW discharge in cartridge mode is a Level 1 force incident. CEW in drive stun mode is Level 2. (P.G. 221-03)

Front:
**3.** Who investigates Level 3 force incidents?

A) A) Immediate Supervisor
B) B) Commanding Officer/Executive Officer
C) C) Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) Force Investigation Division
Back:
**Answer: C**

Level 3 force incidents (force readily capable of causing death/serious injury, chokehold, excessive force with serious injury) are investigated by the Internal Affairs Bureau. (P.G. 221-03)

Front:
**4.** The immediate supervisor in a force incident must be:

A) A) Any available supervisor from the same command
B) B) A supervisor not involved in the incident, at least one rank higher than all involved members
C) C) The desk officer of the precinct of occurrence
D) D) The duty captain in all cases
Back:
**Answer: B**

The immediate supervisor must be available, NOT involved in the incident, from the same command, and at least one rank higher than all involved members. (P.G. 221-03)

Front:
**5.** After a firearms discharge causing death or injury, the involved UMOS is temporarily assigned for a minimum of:

A) A) One scheduled tour
B) B) Three consecutive scheduled tours
C) C) Five consecutive scheduled tours
D) D) Seven calendar days
Back:
**Answer: B**

The involved member is temporarily assigned to the patrol borough office for a minimum of three consecutive scheduled tours (exclusive of sick time or RDOs). (P.G. 221-04)

Front:
**6.** O.C. pepper spray should be applied using:

A) A) One continuous burst at two feet
B) B) Two, one-second bursts at a minimum distance of three feet
C) C) Three short bursts at five feet
D) D) One long burst at arm's length
Back:
**Answer: B**

O.C. pepper spray is held upright, aimed at the subject's eyes, using two one-second bursts at a minimum distance of three feet. (P.G. 221-07)

Front:
**7.** What is the recommended minimum "zone of safety" distance from an EDP?

A) A) 10 feet
B) B) 15 feet
C) C) 20 feet
D) D) 25 feet
Back:
**Answer: C**

A minimum distance of 20 feet is recommended for the zone of safety. The distance should be greater than the effective range of the weapon (other than a firearm). (P.G. 221-13)

Front:
**8.** The UMOS Report of Firearms Discharge (PD324-140) must be completed within:

A) A) 24 hours
B) B) 48 hours
C) C) 72 hours
D) D) End of tour
Back:
**Answer: B**

The PD324-140 must be completed within 48 hours of the incident. (P.G. 221-04)

Front:
**9.** Who has the authority to approve the use of tear gas?

A) A) Precinct commanding officer
B) B) Patrol borough commander or designee
C) C) Chief of Department
D) D) Emergency Service Unit supervisor
Back:
**Answer: B**

The ultimate decision concerning use of tear gas rests with the patrol borough commander or designee. Technical decisions are made by the ranking ESU member. (P.G. 221-09)

Front:
**10.** Members of the service have a duty to intervene to stop excessive force. Failure to intervene may result in:

A) A) A verbal reprimand
B) B) Command discipline only
C) C) Criminal and civil liability and Department discipline up to dismissal
D) D) Transfer to another command
Back:
**Answer: C**

Failure to intervene in or report excessive force is serious misconduct that may result in criminal and civil liability and Department discipline up to and including dismissal. (P.G. 221-02)

Front:
**11.** A "Resisting Recidivist" is defined as a prisoner arrested for charges against MOS:

A) A) Three or more times in ten years
B) B) More than once within five years
C) C) Twice within one year
D) D) Any repeat offense regardless of timeframe
Back:
**Answer: B**

A Resisting Recidivist is a prisoner arrested for resisting arrest, assault on PO, or similar charges more than once within five years (i.e., the second time in five years). The detective squad must enhance the arrest. (P.G. 221-06)

Front:
**12.** The use of distraction devices requires approval from:

A) A) Patrol borough commander
B) B) Duty inspector
C) C) Chief of Department or designee
D) D) Chief of Patrol
Back:
**Answer: C**

Distraction devices require direct knowledge and specific approval of the Chief of Department (or designee), absent exigent circumstances. (P.G. 221-11)

Front:
**13.** Who may apply a spit hood to an EDP?

A) A) Any uniformed member of the service
B) B) The patrol supervisor
C) C) Only Emergency Service Unit personnel
D) D) EMS personnel only
Back:
**Answer: C**

Only ESU personnel may apply a spit hood. Only ESU personnel or a medical professional with minimum EMT certification can remove it. (P.G. 221-13)

Front:
**14.** CEWs should be carried in a holster on which side?

A) A) Shooting side, same as firearm
B) B) Support side, opposite the firearm
C) C) Either side at the member's preference
D) D) Ankle holster
Back:
**Answer: B**

The CEW holster must be attached to the duty belt on the support side, opposite the firearm. Cross-drawing is prohibited. (P.G. 221-08)

Front:
**15.** An EDP must NEVER be transported to:

A) A) The nearest hospital
B) B) A police facility
C) C) A psychiatric ward
D) D) An ambulance
Back:
**Answer: B**

Under no circumstances will an EDP be transported to a police facility. EDPs are removed to a hospital via ambulance (or RMP if ambulance unavailable, at supervisor's direction). (P.G. 221-13)

Front:
**16.** At the scene of a firearms discharge, the PBT alcohol test threshold is:

A) A) .04
B) B) .05
C) C) .08
D) D) .10
Back:
**Answer: C**

If the PBT reading is less than .08 and no indicia of intoxication, no further testing is required. At .08 or greater, an Intoxilyzer test at the IDTU facility is required. (P.G. 221-23)

Front:
**17.** Which of the following is NOT an authorized use of O.C. pepper spray?

A) A) Gaining control of a person actively resisting arrest
B) B) Preventing an individual from injuring themselves
C) C) Controlling a subject who is passively resisting
D) D) Controlling a dangerous animal
Back:
**Answer: C**

O.C. pepper spray, CEW, and impact weapons should NOT be used on persons who are passively resisting. (P.G. 221-07)

Front:
**18.** Vehicle checkpoints should NOT exceed what duration at one location?

A) A) 1 hour
B) B) 2 hours
C) C) 3 hours
D) D) 4 hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

A vehicle checkpoint should not exceed two hours at one location, regardless of conditions. (P.G. 221-16)

Front:
**19.** Department canine teams shall NOT be used for:

A) A) Tracking missing persons
B) B) Crowd control or deterrent at peaceful demonstrations
C) C) Searching buildings for break-in suspects
D) D) Performing cadaver searches
Back:
**Answer: B**

Canine teams will not be used for crowd control or as a deterrent at peaceful demonstrations. Use at non-peaceful demonstrations requires prior approval of the Chief of Patrol. (P.G. 221-18)

Front:
**20.** When a UMOS discharges a firearm outside of New York City, the Operations Unit must be notified within:

A) A) 1 hour
B) B) 6 hours
C) C) 12 hours
D) D) 24 hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

If a firearm is discharged outside of NYC, the member must notify local police authorities immediately and the Operations Unit within 6 hours of discharge. (P.G. 221-04)

Front:
**21.** A CEW standard cycle is how many seconds?

A) A) 3 seconds
B) B) 5 seconds
C) C) 10 seconds
D) D) 15 seconds
Back:
**Answer: B**

Members should use a CEW for one standard cycle of five seconds while constantly assessing. Exposure beyond 15 seconds may increase the risk of death or serious injury. (P.G. 221-08)

Front:
**22.** The response of Emergency Service Unit to an EDP scene may only be canceled by:

A) A) Any member of the service
B) B) The desk officer
C) C) A uniformed supervisor in the rank of sergeant or above
D) D) The precinct commanding officer only
Back:
**Answer: C**

The response of ESU may only be canceled by a uniformed supervisor in the rank of sergeant or above. (P.G. 221-13)

Front:
**23.** Members SHALL NOT discharge firearms at or from a moving vehicle unless:

A) A) The vehicle is being used in a pursuit
B) B) The vehicle is stolen
C) C) Deadly physical force is being used by means other than a moving vehicle
D) D) The vehicle is approaching a checkpoint
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members SHALL NOT discharge firearms at or from a moving vehicle unless deadly physical force is being used against MOS or another person by means OTHER than a moving vehicle. (P.G. 221-01)

Front:
**24.** How many T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORTS are prepared per force incident?

A) A) One per involved member
B) B) One per subject
C) C) Only ONE per incident, regardless of number of MOS or subjects
D) D) One per force level
Back:
**Answer: C**

Only one T.R.I. INCIDENT REPORT will be prepared for an incident, regardless of the number of MOS involved or number of subjects/civilians/bystanders involved. (P.G. 221-03)

Front:
**25.** For Level 3 and Level 4 force incidents, the immediate supervisor should handle body-worn camera footage by:

A) A) Reviewing all videos and documenting findings
B) B) Uploading footage to FORMS T.R.I. application
C) C) Safeguarding cameras and NOT viewing — await IAB/FID direction
D) D) Forwarding footage to the commanding officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

Body-worn camera footage concerning Level 3 and Level 4 force incidents will NOT be viewed by the immediate supervisor. Immediate supervisors will safeguard the cameras and await direction from IAB or FID. (P.G. 221-03)

Front:
**3.** The Borough Executive Officer, Operations supervises which of the following?

A) A) Borough Administrator and Borough Adjutant
B) B) Borough Operations Commander, Borough Special Projects Coordinator, and Borough specialty units
C) C) Borough Adjutant and Borough Operations Commander
D) D) Borough Administrator and Borough Operations Commander
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Borough Executive Officer, Operations supervises the Borough Operations Commander, Borough Special Projects Coordinator, and Borough specialty units. (A.G. 303-02, duty #1)

Front:
**4.** Which borough executive officer is responsible for oversight of discipline and the evaluation process?

A) A) Borough Executive Officer, Operations
B) B) Borough Commander
C) C) Borough Executive Officer, Administration
D) D) Borough Adjutant
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Borough Executive Officer, Administration provides operational and administrative oversight for discipline and personnel issues including the evaluation process. (A.G. 303-03, duty #2c, #2e(2))

Front:
**5.** The Borough Adjutant reports to which position?

A) A) Borough Commander
B) B) Borough Executive Officer, Operations
C) C) Borough Executive Officer, Administration
D) D) Borough Operations Commander
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Borough Adjutant reports to the Borough Executive Officer, Administration. (A.G. 303-04, duty #1)

Front:
**6.** Which of the following is NOT a responsibility of the Borough Adjutant?

A) A) Participate in Early Intervention Committee
B) B) Monitor sick records with attention to Chronic Absent members
C) C) Supervise special event preparation
D) D) Review CCRB conferral reports for accuracy and completeness
Back:
**Answer: C**

Supervising special event preparation is a duty of the Borough Operations Commander (303-05), not the Borough Adjutant. (A.G. 303-04 and 303-05)

Front:
**7.** The Borough Operations Commander develops crime reduction strategies for how many specific crime categories?

A) A) 5
B) B) 7
C) C) 9
D) D) 11
Back:
**Answer: C**

Nine categories: sex crimes, robberies, assaults, burglaries, larcenies/thefts, vehicle theft, domestic violence, firearms violence, and other crimes/conditions as they emerge. (A.G. 303-05, duty #3)

Front:
**8.** The Borough Executive Officer, Operations must liaison with which coordinator regarding emergency preparedness and mobilizations?

A) A) Borough Special Projects Coordinator
B) B) Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator
C) C) Borough Administrator
D) D) Borough Adjutant
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Borough Executive Officer, Operations must liaison with the Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator to ensure operational readiness of emergency preparedness and mobilizations. (A.G. 303-02, duty #4)

Front:
**9.** How often must the Borough Commander convene meetings with members of the borough's executive staff and the Department's line organizations?

A) A) Monthly
B) B) Semi-annually
C) C) Quarterly (or as needed)
D) D) Annually
Back:
**Answer: C**

Quarterly (or as needed) to discuss command level management issues and resolve challenges faced by members on patrol. (A.G. 303-01, duty #9)

Front:
**10.** The Borough Operations Commander oversees the selection of command youth coordination officers after conferral with whom?

A) A) Borough Commander
B) B) Borough Executive Officer, Operations
C) C) Precinct/PSA commanding officers
D) D) Community Affairs Bureau
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Borough Operations Commander oversees selection of command youth coordination officers after conferral with precinct/PSA commanding officers. (A.G. 303-05, duty #5)

Front:
**1.** A uniformed member of the service who misuses a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive alcohol consumption will be:

A) A) Placed on modified assignment
B) B) Suspended for 30 days
C) C) Terminated from the Department
D) D) Given command discipline
Back:
**Answer: C**

Any misconduct involving a member's misuse of a firearm while unfit for duty due to excessive consumption of and intoxication from alcohol will result in termination. Exceptional cases are determined by the Police Commissioner on a case-by-case basis. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**2.** A UMOS causes serious physical injury to another person while operating a motor vehicle and is determined to be unfit for duty due to alcohol. What is the consequence?

A) A) Dismissal Probation only
B) B) Suspension and retraining
C) C) Termination, absent exigent circumstances
D) D) Modified assignment for 30 days
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Department policy states that any UMOS who causes serious physical injury while operating a motor vehicle and is determined unfit for duty due to alcohol consumption will be terminated, absent exigent circumstances. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**3.** A UMOS refuses to submit to chemical testing in connection with an alleged violation of VTL §1192 (DWI). The member will be charged with violating:

A) A) A.G. 304-04, "Fitness For Duty"
B) B) A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct"
C) C) A.G. 318-10, "Removal of Firearms"
D) D) P.G. 221-23, "Alcohol Testing"
Back:
**Answer: B**

A UMOS who refuses chemical testing for alleged DWI will be charged with violating A.G. 304-06, "Prohibited Conduct" — engaging in conduct prejudicial to the good order, efficiency, or discipline of the Department. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**4.** The Counseling Services Unit maintains confidentiality standards under:

A) A) New York State Civil Service Law
B) B) Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs
C) C) Department Administrative Guide only
D) D) HIPAA regulations
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Counseling Services Unit maintains strict standards of confidentiality as set forth in Title 42 of Federal guidelines governing alcoholism programs. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**5.** A member participating in counseling programs for alcohol-related problems should be aware that:

A) A) Their promotional opportunities will be jeopardized
B) B) They will automatically be transferred
C) C) Participation will NOT jeopardize promotional opportunities
D) D) They will be placed on restricted duty
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members participating in these programs will not jeopardize their promotional opportunities, and current assignments will not ordinarily be changed. However, participation does not exempt a member from disciplinary action for specific acts of misconduct. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**6.** Any negotiated penalty for a UMOS determined to have been operating a motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol shall include:

A) A) A verbal reprimand
B) B) A period of Dismissal Probation
C) C) A 10-day suspension
D) D) Command discipline only
Back:
**Answer: B**

Any negotiated penalty involving a UMOS operating a motor vehicle while unfit due to alcohol shall include a period of Dismissal Probation. The member must also agree to submit to ordered breath testing during the probation period. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**7.** POPPA is best described as:

A) A) A Department disciplinary unit
B) B) A joint labor-management, union-operated employee assistance program
C) C) A civilian oversight board
D) D) An Internal Affairs Bureau division
Back:
**Answer: B**

POPPA (Police Organization Providing Peer Assistance) is a joint labor-management cooperative effort involving the implementation of a union-operated, alternative employee assistance program. Both HELPLINE and POPPA offer confidential services. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**8.** The Counseling Services Unit operates:

A) A) Monday through Friday, 0900-1700 hours
B) B) 24 hours a day, seven days a week
C) C) Only during business hours
D) D) By appointment only
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Counseling Services Unit operates 24 hours a day, seven days a week, and will help assess the extent of the problem and make appropriate referrals for treatment. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**9.** Which of the following correctly states the fitness-for-duty requirement?

A) A) Be fit for duty at all times
B) B) Be fit for duty at all times, except when on sick report
C) C) Be fit for duty at all times, except when off duty
D) D) Be fit for duty while on patrol only
Back:
**Answer: B**

The qualifier "except when on sick report" is critical. Answer choice A is a trap — it omits the qualifier and is therefore incorrect. Always look for the complete statement with the "EXCEPT" qualifier. (A.G. 304-04)

Front:
**10.** A member of the service who serves on a Community Board may:

A) A) Serve on the Public Safety Committee
B) B) Vote on PD activities in the district the board serves
C) C) Serve on the board but NOT on the Public Safety Committee
D) D) Not serve on a Community Board at all
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members CAN serve on a Community Board, but CANNOT serve on the Community Board's Public Safety Committee if it deals with PD and other law enforcement matters. They also cannot vote on Community Board matters regarding PD activities in the district the board serves. (A.G. 304-06)

Front:
**11.** When entering a command, a uniformed member should salute:

A) A) The Desk Officer
B) B) The Commanding Officer
C) C) The Desk
D) D) The highest-ranking officer present
Back:
**Answer: C**

When entering the command, members salute The DESK — not the Desk Officer. The salute is rendered to the desk itself as a symbol of command authority. (A.G. 304-02)

Front:
**12.** A breathalyzer result of what level or greater constitutes "unfit for duty"?

A) A) .02
B) B) .04
C) C) .06
D) D) .08
Back:
**Answer: B**

A breathalyzer result of .04 or greater means the member is unfit for duty. This is lower than the legal DWI limit of .08 — the Department holds members to a stricter standard. (A.G. 318-20, note after step 1)

Front:
**13.** A member's driver's license is suspended. Who must the member notify?

A) A) Immediate supervisor
B) B) Desk Officer
C) C) Commanding Officer
D) D) IAB directly
Back:
**Answer: C**

The member must notify the Commanding Officer directly if their license is suspended, revoked, or not renewed. This breaks the normal chain of command — the notification goes straight to the C.O. (A.G. 304-03)

Front:
**14.** Locker stickers on the OUTSIDE of a member's locker include:

A) A) Gun Safety and Seat Belt
B) B) Police Don't Move and Health and Wellness
C) C) Police Don't Move and Proper Tactics Save Lives
D) D) Proper Tactics Save Lives and Gun Safety
Back:
**Answer: C**

Two stickers go on the OUTSIDE: "Police Don't Move" (with rank, name, shield, squad) and "Proper Tactics Save Lives." Three go on the INSIDE: Gun Safety, Seat Belt, and Health and Wellness. (A.G. 304-07)

Front:
**15.** A member is nominated to an elected office. How many days does the member have to decline before automatically vacating their Department position?

A) A) 5 days
B) B) 10 days
C) C) 15 days
D) D) 30 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

If a member accepts or is nominated to elected office, the MOS has 10 days to turn it down or automatically vacate their position with the Department. Exceptions include Community Board, Board of Education outside the city, or unpaid leave with the Mayor's permission. (A.G. 304-06)

Front:
**16.** The medical exemption for facial hair (Shaving Restriction Form) is valid for:

A) A) 90 days
B) B) 120 days
C) C) 180 days
D) D) 1 year
Back:
**Answer: C**

The medical exemption for facial hair is ONLY good for 180 days. Reevaluation is required to extend it. Members should schedule an appointment to renew the Shaving Restriction Form 1 month prior to expiration. (A.G. 304-08)

Front:
**1.** A UMOS may be unarmed at their own discretion while off duty in all of the following situations EXCEPT:

A) A) On vacation
B) B) Engaged in authorized off-duty employment
C) C) Walking through a NYC neighborhood
D) D) There is a likelihood of consuming alcohol
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members must be armed at all times when in NYC, unless specific exceptions apply: risk of loss/theft (sporting activities, beach), vacation, authorized off-duty employment, activity where it would be inadvisable to carry, or likelihood of consuming alcohol. Walking through NYC does not qualify. (A.G. 305-07)

Front:
**2.** Which type of holster is prohibited under A.G. 305-07?

A) A) Shoulder holster
B) B) Ankle holster
C) C) Belly band holster
D) D) Inside-waistband holster
Back:
**Answer: C**

"Belly band" holsters, "clip-on" holsters, and "one size fits all" holsters are prohibited. Shoulder and ankle holsters are permitted if they meet retention requirements. (A.G. 305-07)

Front:
**3.** Firearms may NOT be stored or left in:

A) A) A locked firearms safe at home
B) B) An unattended motor vehicle
C) C) The desk officer's safekeeping
D) D) A firearms safety station
Back:
**Answer: B**

Members shall not store or leave firearms in an unattended motor vehicle, except as authorized (e.g., P.G. 221-10). (A.G. 305-07)

Front:
**4.** All firearm repairs and modifications must be performed by:

A) A) Any certified gunsmith
B) B) The member's commanding officer
C) C) Firearms and Tactics Section armorers solely
D) D) The member with CO approval
Back:
**Answer: C**

All repairs and modifications to authorized firearms must be performed solely by Firearms and Tactics Section armorers. The member must pass a qualification course before carrying a modified firearm. (A.G. 305-07)

Front:
**5.** A report for lost or damaged uniform must be filed within:

A) A) 10 days
B) B) 30 days
C) C) 60 days
D) D) 90 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

The report must be prepared on Typed Letterhead addressed to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget, within 30 days from the date of incident. (A.G. 305-05)

Front:
**6.** Reimbursement for lost or damaged uniform items costing less than $100 should be forwarded to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget:

A) A) Through channels
B) B) Direct
C) C) Via Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) Through the Chief of Department
Back:
**Answer: B**

Items costing less than $100 are forwarded direct to the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. Items costing $100 or more go through channels. (A.G. 305-05)

Front:
**7.** The maximum reimbursement amount for a lost or damaged watch is:

A) A) $50.00
B) B) $75.00
C) C) $100.00
D) D) $150.00
Back:
**Answer: B**

A maximum of $75.00 has been established as a reasonable amount for watch reimbursement. Members are reminded they must be equipped with a serviceable watch while performing patrol duty. (A.G. 305-05)

Front:
**8.** A UMOS entering Family Court must be:

A) A) Armed with service pistol only
B) B) Unarmed, unless present as arresting officer or on official business
C) C) Armed with both service and off-duty weapons
D) D) Armed but with weapon concealed
Back:
**Answer: B**

Members must be unarmed when entering Family Court or Supreme Court Matrimonial Parts, unless involved as arresting officer or on official business in a case. If armed as arresting officer, report to court officer supervisor and sign the Law Enforcement Officer log. (A.G. 305-07)

Front:
**9.** All settlements for lost or damaged uniform reimbursement are non-transferable and must be executed within:

A) A) 90 days of approval
B) B) 120 days of approval
C) C) 180 days of approval
D) D) One year of approval
Back:
**Answer: C**

All settlements are non-transferable and MUST be executed within 180 days of approval by the Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget. (A.G. 305-05)

Front:
**10.** A UMOS carrying an authorized firearm into a foreign country (not on official business or sporting event) will face:

A) A) A verbal warning
B) B) No consequences if firearm is properly secured
C) C) Disciplinary action and possibly criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation
D) D) Modified assignment
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members are prohibited from carrying firearms into a foreign country unless on official business or personally participating in an event requiring use of a firearm. Failure to comply will result in disciplinary action and possibly subject the member to criminal penalties and/or weapon confiscation. (A.G. 305-07)

Front:
**11.** A Sergeant's nylon windbreaker displays rank insignia in which location?

A) A) Chevrons on both sleeves
B) B) Insignia on collar only
C) C) Chevrons on the left sleeve only
D) D) Gold letters across the back
Back:
**Answer: B**

On the nylon windbreaker, Sergeants have insignia on the collar only — NO chevrons on the sleeves. This is different from the V-neck sweater, where Sergeants DO wear chevrons on the sleeves. (A.G. 305-04)

Front:
**12.** The WTC Memorial breast bar may be worn by:

A) A) Only UMOS who were on the job on 9/11/2001
B) B) Only UMOS assigned to WTC recovery operations
C) C) All UMOS, regardless of whether they were on the job on 9/11
D) D) Only UMOS who received a commendation for 9/11 service
Back:
**Answer: C**

The WTC Memorial breast bar may be worn by all UMOS above all other bars except the US Flag bar. Unlike anniversary bars, members did NOT have to be on the job during 9/11 to wear it. (A.G. 305-13)

Front:
**13.** A protective vest must meet what minimum threat level certification?

A) A) Threat Level I
B) B) Threat Level II
C) C) Exceeding Threat Level II (Level III or higher)
D) D) Threat Level IV
Back:
**Answer: C**

The vest must be certified by the NIJ as EXCEEDING Threat Level II — meaning Level II itself is NOT acceptable. The vest must be Level III or higher. Vests made of Zylon are also NOT authorized. (A.G. 305-15)

Front:
**14.** When a member dies in the line of duty, members of his/her command wear the mourning band until:

A) A) The day of the funeral
B) B) 2400 hrs on the 10th day after death
C) C) 10 days after the funeral
D) D) 2400 hrs on the day of the funeral
Back:
**Answer: B**

For LOD death, members of the deceased's command wear the mourning band from time of death until 2400 hrs on the 10th day after death. Other members wear it from time of death until 2400 hours on the day of the funeral. Note that the flag at half-mast period uses "10 days after funeral" as its reference — a different timeline. (A.G. 305-14 / A.G. 304-02)

Front:
**15.** On the duty belt, OC pepper spray is carried on which side?

A) A) Strong side, next to firearm
B) B) Support side, opposite the firearm
C) C) Center line of the belt
D) D) Either side at the member's discretion
Back:
**Answer: B**

OC pepper spray device and holder are carried on the support side (non-shooting side), opposite the firearm. It is item #4 on the support side of the belt. (A.G. 305-08)

Front:
**16.** Who must a supervisor contact for a reserve protective vest supply?

A) A) The Equipment Section
B) B) The member's assigned bureau
C) C) The local Patrol Borough
D) D) The Police Commissioner's office
Back:
**Answer: C**

Regardless of the member's bureau (Housing, Transit, Detective Bureau, etc.), the supervisor directs the requesting MOS to call the local Patrol Borough for reserve vest supply. If the Patrol Borough does not have any, contact the PA Vest Unit. (A.G. 305-15)

Front:
**17.** The mnemonic "A FAVOR" helps remember when a member may be unarmed off duty. What does the "F" stand for?

A) A) Fitness activities
B) B) Family Court or Supreme Court Matrimonial
C) C) Foreign country travel
D) D) Formal Department functions
Back:
**Answer: B**

A FAVOR: A = Alcohol likely consumed; F = Family or Supreme Court Matrimonial (don't enter armed except as arresting officer or on official business); A = Advisable not to carry; V = Vacation; O = Off-Duty Employment; R = Risk of Loss or Theft (sports, beach, etc.). (A.G. 305-07)

Front:
**1.** Upon observing a UMOS who appears unfit for duty due to intoxication, the supervisory member must prepare the SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150):

A) A) Within 24 hours
B) B) By end of tour
C) C) Immediately
D) D) Within 48 hours
Back:
**Answer: C**

The supervisory member must prepare the PD469-150 immediately based upon observations of the member. The CO/XO/duty captain must also prepare their own PD469-150 immediately. (A.G. 318-10)

Front:
**2.** When a UMOS is determined to be intoxicated, the CO/XO/duty captain should:

A) A) Issue a verbal warning and send member home
B) B) Remove firearms, place on modified assignment or suspend, and advise of counseling
C) C) Notify Internal Affairs Bureau only
D) D) Contact the Police Commissioner directly
Back:
**Answer: B**

The CO/XO/duty captain removes firearms (per A.G. 318-13), places member on modified assignment or suspends, advises of Counseling Service Programs, and mandates supervisory officer contact to Counseling Services Unit. (A.G. 318-10)

Front:
**3.** The Typed Letterhead report for an intoxicated UMOS must be prepared in how many copies and forwarded to how many recipients?

A) A) Five copies, five recipients
B) B) Six copies, six recipients
C) C) Eight copies, eight recipients
D) D) Ten copies, ten recipients
Back:
**Answer: C**

Eight copies are prepared and forwarded to: First Deputy Commissioner (original, DIRECT + through channels), Chief of Department, Chief of Personnel, Chief of Internal Affairs, Deputy Commissioner Trials, Department Advocate's Office, and member's CO — all DIRECT. (A.G. 318-10)

Front:
**4.** Prior to questioning a member who is the subject of an official investigation, the interrogating officer must provide:

A) A) CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS (PD468-121)
B) B) NOTIFICATION OF ALLEGATION(S) AND EMPLOYEE'S RIGHTS (PD339-121)
C) C) SUPERVISOR'S FITNESS FOR DUTY REPORT (PD469-150)
D) D) Miranda Warning Card
Back:
**Answer: B**

Prior to questioning, the interrogating officer must provide the member a completed PD339-121 for review and endorsement, allowing reasonable time for review. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**5.** A member who is the subject of an official investigation has the right to view which recording prior to questioning?

A) A) Any CCTV footage from the precinct
B) B) Their own Body-Worn Camera recording related to the incident
C) C) All BWC recordings from all officers at the scene
D) D) No recordings may be viewed
Back:
**Answer: B**

The member will be permitted to view their own BWC recording related to the incident(s) under investigation prior to questioning at a time and place deemed appropriate by the supervisor in charge of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**6.** A UMOS who refuses to answer questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties will be:

A) A) Given a verbal reprimand
B) B) Placed on modified assignment
C) C) Subject to suspension from duty
D) D) Charged with insubordination only
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members must answer questions specifically directed and narrowly related to official duties. Refusal shall result in suspension from duty. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**7.** Under the Garrity warning, a member's statements during an official investigation:

A) A) Can be used in both criminal proceedings and departmental charges
B) B) Cannot be used in criminal proceedings but CAN be used in departmental charges
C) C) Cannot be used in either criminal proceedings or departmental charges
D) D) Can be used in criminal proceedings but NOT departmental charges
Back:
**Answer: B**

If the member answers, neither their statements nor any information gained by reason of such statements can be used against them in subsequent criminal proceedings. However, the statements may be used against them in relation to subsequent departmental charges. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**8.** An interrogation conducted after CHARGES AND SPECIFICATIONS have been served must be completed at least how many days prior to the Department trial date?

A) A) 5 days
B) B) 10 days
C) C) 15 days
D) D) 20 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

An interrogation conducted after service of charges must be completed at least 10 days prior to the date of Department trial, except as directed by the Deputy Commissioner - Trials. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**9.** The determination of what constitutes a "reasonable time" for a member to obtain counsel before interrogation is made by:

A) A) The member's commanding officer
B) B) The desk officer
C) C) The captain (or above) in charge of the investigation
D) D) The member's union representative
Back:
**Answer: C**

In all cases, the determination as to what is a reasonable time will be made by the captain (or above) in charge of the investigation, considering the nature of the investigation, timeliness, and stage of the investigation. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**10.** A supervisory officer is mandated to contact the Counseling Services Unit when a member is:

A) A) Placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has firearms removed due to unfitness
B) B) Under criminal investigation only
C) C) Transferred to another command
D) D) Issued command discipline
Back:
**Answer: A**

A supervisory officer is mandated in all cases to contact the Counseling Services Unit on behalf of a member who is placed on modified assignment, suspended, or has their firearms removed due to being unfit for duty. (A.G. 318-10)

Front:
**11.** Which of the following is a prohibited interrogation tactic?

A) A) Recording the interrogation mechanically
B) B) Having a line organization representative present
C) C) Promises of reward for answering questions
D) D) Scheduling interrogation during business hours
Back:
**Answer: C**

Prohibited tactics include: "off the record" questions, offensive language or threats (transfer, dismissal, disciplinary punishment), and promises of reward for answering questions. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**12.** Questions and answers from an official investigation interrogation may be released outside the Department only with written approval of:

A) A) The Police Commissioner
B) B) The Chief of Department
C) C) The Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters
D) D) The Chief of Internal Affairs
Back:
**Answer: C**

Questions and answers resulting from interrogation are confidential and not to be revealed or released outside the Department without prior written approval of the Deputy Commissioner - Legal Matters. If a subpoena duces tecum is received, contact the Legal Bureau immediately. (A.G. 318-11)

Front:
**13.** The maximum penalty for a Schedule "A" Command Discipline is:

A) A) 3 days
B) B) 5 days
C) C) 10 days
D) D) 20 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

The three types of CDs have different maximums: "A" = 5 days, "B" = 10 days, "C" = 20 days. "C" CDs are issued only by the Department Advocate. (A.G. 318-01)

Front:
**14.** When a CD results from an IAB investigation and the CO wants to change the recommended disciplinary action, the CO must obtain conferral and approval from:

A) A) The investigating entity
B) B) The Deputy Commissioner, Department Advocate
C) C) The Chief of IAB
D) D) The Patrol Borough Adjutant
Back:
**Answer: B**

When a CD comes from IAB: to change findings, you need approval of the investigating entity. To change discipline, you need approval of the DC Department Advocate. The exam often switches these two. (A.G. 318-02)

Front:
**15.** The CD Review Panel may increase the penalty proposed by the CO to not more than:

A) A) The same penalty proposed
B) B) One and a half times the proposed penalty
C) C) Double the proposed penalty
D) D) Triple the proposed penalty
Back:
**Answer: C**
