The CD Review Panel may approve, reduce, or increase the penalty — but the increase cannot exceed double what the CO proposed. (A.G. 318-02)

Front:
**16.** A Uniformed Sergeant must suspend an MOS for all of the following EXCEPT:

A) A) Arrest for a misdemeanor
B) B) Refusal to take a Dole Test
C) C) Being impaired (BAC under .08)
D) D) AWOL for 5 consecutive tours
Back:
**Answer: C**

The mandatory suspension grounds (A DOG CAR) include DWI (VTL 1192 subs 2-4a) but NOT impairment below .08. By its absence from the list, impaired is not a must-suspend. (A.G. 318-05)

Front:
**17.** A member must elect one of the three CD options within what timeframe of receiving the CO/XO's decision?

A) A) 3 calendar days
B) B) 3 working days
C) C) 5 working days
D) D) 5 calendar days
Back:
**Answer: B**

The member must notify the CO in writing on the CD of the option elected within 3 working days of receiving the CO/XO's decision. When the AG does not specify "working," it means calendar days. (A.G. 318-02)

Front:
**18.** Records and dispositions of substantiated Schedule "B" CDs may be sealed after:

A) A) 1 year from issuance with no subsequent discipline
B) B) 3 years from issuance with no subsequent "B" or Charges
C) C) 3 years from disposition, upon written request, with no subsequent "B" or Charges
D) D) They are automatically sealed after 3 years
Back:
**Answer: C**

Sealing of "B" CDs is NOT automatic — the member must request in writing via a 49 to their CO on the 3rd anniversary of the date of disposition. If no subsequent "B" CDs or Charges during those 3 years, the CO expunges from the command folder. (A.G. 318-12/318-14)

Front:
**19.** When an MOS is arrested outside the city, who designates the Captain or above to conduct the investigation?

A) A) The Patrol Borough CO
B) B) The Duty Captain
C) C) The Chief (of Department)
D) D) The MOS's Commanding Officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

When arrested outside the city, there is no "Duty Captain" — the Chief designates a Captain or above to conduct the investigation. Inside the city, the Borough CO assigns the Ranking Officer. (A.G. 318-09)

Front:
**20.** Loss of a Department-issued smartphone results in which type of Command Discipline?

A) A) Schedule "A" CD
B) B) Schedule "B" CD
C) C) Schedule "C" CD
D) D) Charges and Specifications
Back:
**Answer: A**

Loss of an ID Card, Summons, Summons Book, or Department-issued smartphone are all Schedule "A" CDs. Loss of shield, Activity Log, prisoner (failure to safeguard), or other Department property (except the "A" items) are Schedule "B" CDs. (A.G. 318-01)

Front:
**1.** A civilian member must report an on-duty injury no later than:

A) A) 24 hours after the incident
B) B) 3 days after the incident
C) C) 7 days after the incident
D) D) 30 days after the incident
Back:
**Answer: C**

The civilian member must report injury to supervisor at time of incident, and the injury must be reported no later than 7 days after the incident. (A.G. 319-08)

Front:
**2.** The investigating supervisor must notify the Civilian Employee Services Unit and forward copies within:

A) A) Immediately by telephone; copies within 12 hours
B) B) Immediately by telephone during business hours; copies within 24 hours
C) C) By end of tour; copies within 48 hours
D) D) Within 24 hours; copies within 72 hours
Back:
**Answer: B**

The supervisor must notify the Civilian Employee Services Unit immediately by telephone during business hours (or at 0900 following business day) and forward copies within 24 hours of notification. (A.G. 319-08)

Front:
**3.** Who is empowered to grant leave with pay for the first week's absence of a civilian member covered by Workers Compensation?

A) A) The commanding officer
B) B) The desk officer
C) C) The Chief of Personnel
D) D) The Deputy Commissioner, Management and Budget
Back:
**Answer: C**

The Chief of Personnel is empowered to grant leave with pay for the first week's absence of a member covered by Workers Compensation who is physically disabled in the performance of official duties. (A.G. 319-08)

Front:
**4.** How many forms must the investigating supervisor ensure are prepared when a civilian member is injured on duty?

A) A) 3
B) B) 4
C) C) 5
D) D) 6
Back:
**Answer: C**

Five forms: Employee Statement Form (FISA WCS-110), Supervisor's Report of Injury (FISA WCS-100), Witness Statement Form (FISA WCS-120), Employee Option Form (DP2002), and Time Usage 49. (A.G. 319-08)

Front:
**1.** An exchange of tour (mutual) must be performed within the same:

A) A) Calendar month
B) B) 28-day FLSA cycle
C) C) Pay period
D) D) Calendar week
Back:
**Answer: B**

Exchange of tours must be performed in the same twenty-eight day Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) cycle. (A.G. 320-01)

Front:
**2.** A uniformed member of the service requesting an exchange of tours must submit the EXCHANGE OF TOUR REQUEST to the operations coordinator at least how many days in advance?

A) A) 3 days
B) B) 5 days
C) C) 7 days
D) D) 10 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

The request must be submitted at least five days in advance. (A.G. 320-01, step #5)

Front:
**3.** During an exchange of tours, a uniformed member of the service who becomes ill must report:

A) A) Administrative Sick
B) B) Regular Sick, which requires a visit to the Department surgeon
C) C) Either Administrative or Regular Sick, at the member's discretion
D) D) Line of Duty Sick
Back:
**Answer: B**

Members are NOT permitted to report Administrative Sick during a mutual and must report Regular Sick, which requires a visit to the Department surgeon. (A.G. 320-01, step #2b)

Front:
**4.** Which of the following is NOT a requirement for an exchange of tours?

A) A) Members must be the same rank
B) B) Members must be assigned to the same command
C) C) Members must be below the rank of captain
D) D) Members must be assigned to the same platoon
Back:
**Answer: D**

Members do not need to be on the same platoon — they must be same rank, same command, below captain, and qualified to perform each other's duties. (A.G. 320-01)

Front:
**5.** If a uniformed member of the service fails to perform an exchanged tour, the Department may reschedule any same-rank MOS without how much notice?

A) A) 12-hour notice
B) B) 24-hour notice
C) C) 48-hour notice
D) D) 72-hour notice
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Department may reschedule any uniformed member of the service of the same rank without twenty-four hour notice. (A.G. 320-01, Additional Data)

Front:
**6.** A member of the service intending to visit an inmate at a correctional facility while off-duty must forward a typed letterhead to the commanding officer at least how many days prior?

A) A) 3 days
B) B) 5 days
C) C) 7 days
D) D) 14 days
Back:
**Answer: C**

At least seven days prior to the intended visit. (A.G. 320-02, step #1)

Front:
**7.** When a member of the service requests to visit an inmate at a correctional facility, the commanding officer must notify which of the following?

A) A) Patrol Borough Commander and Operations Unit
B) B) IAB, Command Center
C) C) Chief of Department and Personnel Bureau
D) D) Community Affairs Bureau and Legal Bureau
Back:
**Answer: B**

The commanding officer must notify IAB and the Command Center of details. (A.G. 320-02, step #2)

Front:
**8.** Reports more than six months old are removed from personal folders on which dates?

A) A) March 1 and September 1
B) B) January 1 and July 1
C) C) The first Monday of each quarter
D) D) The member's anniversary date
Back:
**Answer: B**

Reports more than six months old are removed on January 1 and July 1 each year. (A.G. 320-03, step #4)

Front:
**9.** When a member of command is transferred, the personal folder jacket is forwarded to the new command in what manner?

A) A) Via Department mail, unsealed
B) B) Via registered mail
C) C) In a sealed envelope marked "Confidential"
D) D) Hand-delivered by the transferred member
Back:
**Answer: C**

The folder jacket is forwarded in a sealed envelope marked "Confidential." (A.G. 320-03, step #7)

Front:
**10.** The OVERTIME REPORT (PD138-064) is maintained in which location?

A) A) The member's personal folder
B) B) A separate Time Record Folder (TRF)
C) C) The command's central filing system
D) D) The Borough Administrator's office
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Overtime Report is to be kept in a separate Time Record Folder (TRF), not in the personal folder. (A.G. 320-03, Additional Data)

Front:
**1.** When a UMOS is AWOL for 2 hours at the beginning of the tour and lives within the city, the desk officer should request the D.O. of the member's resident precinct to have whom respond to the residence?

A) A) A police officer
B) B) A detective
C) C) A supervisor
D) D) The ICCU
Back:
**Answer: C**

When a member is AWOL for 2 hours and lives in the city, the D.O. requests the member's resident precinct D.O. to send a boss (supervisor) to the residence. If outside the city, ICCU notifies local PD. (P.G. 324-17)

Front:
**2.** At the 4-hour AWOL mark, the desk officer must notify all of the following EXCEPT:

A) A) C.O./Duty Captain
B) B) Boro/Counterpart
C) C) Sick Desk Supervisor
D) D) IAB Command Center
Back:
**Answer: C**

At 4 hours, the D.O. notifies the C.O./Duty Captain, Boro/Counterpart, Operations, and IAB Command Center. The Sick Desk Supervisor is notified later — when the member is located. (P.G. 324-17)

Front:
**3.** A UMOS has been AWOL for an entire tour. The next day, the member reports to the command for duty. The member:

A) A) May be assigned to duty at the desk officer's discretion
B) B) May be assigned to duty with the platoon commander's approval
C) C) Will not be assigned to duty without prior permission of the Chief of Department
D) D) Will be automatically suspended pending investigation
Back:
**Answer: C**

A member AWOL for an entire tour will NOT be assigned to duty without prior permission of the Chief of Department. (P.G. 324-17)

Front:
**4.** When an AWOL member is located, who among the following must be notified?

A) A) Only Operations and IAB
B) B) Boro Command/Counterpart, Operations, IAB, and Sick Desk Supervisor
C) C) Only the C.O. and IAB
D) D) Only the Boro Commander and Operations
Back:
**Answer: B**

When the AWOL member is located, the C.O./Duty Captain notifies the Boro Command/Counterpart, Operations, IAB, AND the Sick Desk Supervisor. (P.G. 324-17)

Front:
**5.** The AWOL procedure (P.G. 324-17) applies to:

A) A) All members of the service
B) B) Uniformed members only — it does NOT apply to CMOS
C) C) Civilian members only
D) D) Detectives and above only
Back:
**Answer: B**

The AWOL procedure does NOT apply to civilian members of the service (CMOS). (P.G. 324-17)

Front:
**6.** When the C.O. investigates an AWOL and prepares the Typed Letterhead (49), the routing is:

A) A) 1 copy direct to Chief of Department, 1 through channels (boro)
B) B) 2 copies direct to Chief of Department
C) C) 1 copy to IAB, 1 to Chief of Department
D) D) 1 copy to the member's file, 1 to Operations
Back:
**Answer: A**

If the C.O. investigated, 1 copy goes direct to the Chief of Department and 1 goes through channels (boro). If the Duty Captain investigated, 1 direct to COD and 2 copies to the MOS's C.O. (P.G. 324-17)

Front:
**7.** When a member is AWOL for 2 hours and lives outside the city, the desk officer should:

A) A) Request the member's resident precinct D.O. to send a supervisor
B) B) Request ICCU to notify local PD to respond to the residence
C) C) Notify IAB Command Center immediately
D) D) Notify Operations Unit immediately
Back:
**Answer: B**

If the member lives outside the city, the D.O. requests ICCU to notify local PD to respond. If in the city, the resident precinct D.O. sends a supervisor. (P.G. 324-17)

Front:
**1.** Terminal leave for a uniformed member with 20 years of service is computed as:

A) A) 30 calendar days
B) B) One calendar month per 10 years of service
C) C) Two calendar months
D) D) 60 calendar days
Back:
**Answer: B**

One calendar month (28, 30, or 31 days depending on the month) of leave is granted for each 10 years of service. Beyond 20 years, 3 days per completed year and 1 day per completed 4 months. (A.G. 329-06)

Front:
**2.** A Tier 2 uniformed member must report to the Police Pension Fund:

A) A) At least 10 calendar days in advance
B) B) At least 30 calendar days in advance
C) C) Without notice
D) D) At least 60 calendar days in advance
Back:
**Answer: B**

Tier 2 members must report at least 30 calendar days in advance of discontinuance of service. Tier 3 members may report without notice. (A.G. 329-06)

Front:
**3.** A LEOSA Certification Card expires:

A) A) 6 months from date of last range requalification
B) B) 12 months from date of last range requalification
C) C) 24 months from date of retirement
D) D) It does not expire
Back:
**Answer: B**

The LEOSA Certification Card will expire 12 months from the date of the member's last range requalification. The member must have qualified during a regular Department range cycle within the past 12 months. (A.G. 329-06)

Front:
**4.** After commencement of terminal leave, which of the following may occur?

A) A) Member may be granted vacation
B) B) Member may be placed on sick report for any illness
C) C) No leaves of any kind may be granted
D) D) Member may request comp time
Back:
**Answer: C**

No leaves of any kind may be granted after commencement of terminal leave, nor may the member be placed on sick report, unless such sick report results from action taken in the line of duty while on terminal leave. (A.G. 329-06)

Front:
**5.** Resignation bars reinstatement EXCEPT if approved by:

A) A) The Chief of Department within 6 months
B) B) The Police Commissioner within one year
C) C) The First Deputy Commissioner within 2 years
D) D) The Chief of Personnel within 90 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Resignation bars reinstatement except if approved by the Police Commissioner within one year of date of resignation. (A.G. 329-07)

Front:
**6.** When a UMOS resigns, the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW form must be distributed within:

A) A) 24 hours
B) B) 3 business days
C) C) 5 business days
D) D) 10 business days
Back:
**Answer: C**

The commanding officer signs and distributes the RESIGNATION AND EXIT INTERVIEW within five business days. (A.G. 329-07)

Front:
**7.** A resigning member's firearms must be delivered to:

A) A) Manhattan Property Clerk
B) B) Firearms and Tactics Section
C) C) Borough Property Clerk's Office only
D) D) The member's permanent command
Back:
**Answer: C**

The resigning member must deliver firearms with PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE and ACQUISITION OR DISPOSITION OF FIREARMS report to the borough Property Clerk's Office ONLY. (A.G. 329-07)

Front:
**8.** Tier 2 uniformed members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their:

A) A) 60th birthday
B) B) 62nd birthday
C) C) 63rd birthday
D) D) 65th birthday
Back:
**Answer: C**

Tier 2 members are required by law to retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 63rd birthday. Tier 3 members must retire no later than midnight of the eve of their 62nd birthday. (A.G. 329-06)

Front:
**9.** Members on terminal leave are available for duty only during:

A) A) Regular scheduled tours
B) B) Emergency situations
C) C) Programmatic overtime
D) D) Detail assignments
Back:
**Answer: B**

Members on terminal leave, or using accrued time attached to terminal leave, are excluded from excusal limitations and are only available for duty during emergency situations (natural disaster, etc.) or court must-appear notifications. (A.G. 329-06)

Front:
**10.** A UMOS wishing to retain a Department-issued service firearm (issued after 1/15/13) must obtain a NICS check within how many days prior to separation?

A) A) 15 days
B) B) 30 days
C) C) 60 days
D) D) 90 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

Members must obtain a NICS check through a Federal Firearms Licensee (FFL) no more than 30 days prior to separation from the Department. (A.G. 329-06)

Front:
**11.** If a UMOS resigns other than in person, who is assigned to open the member's locker?

A) A) The desk officer
B) B) A sergeant and clerical member of the service
C) C) The commanding officer personally
D) D) Internal Affairs Bureau
Back:
**Answer: B**

The desk officer assigns a sergeant and clerical member of the service to open the member's locker and obtain Department property. (A.G. 329-07)

Front:
**12.** Upon learning of a member's resignation, the Operations Unit notifies all of the following EXCEPT:

A) A) Communications Section
B) B) Internal Affairs Bureau
C) C) Payroll Section
D) D) The member's union representative
Back:
**Answer: D**

Operations Unit notifies: Communications Section (FINEST Message), Internal Affairs Bureau, Payroll Section, Police Pension Fund, and Human Resources Division. The union representative is not on this notification list. (A.G. 329-07)

Front:
**1.** The investigating supervisor for a line of duty injury must sign off on the LOD Injury Report:

A) A) Within 24 hours
B) B) Within 48 hours
C) C) Prior to completion of tour
D) D) By end of the following tour
Back:
**Answer: C**

The investigating supervisor reviews for completeness and accuracy, and signs off electronically prior to completion of tour, absent exceptional circumstances. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**2.** For a line of duty injury occurring outside of New York City, the investigating supervisor must be in the rank of:

A) A) Sergeant or above
B) B) Lieutenant or above
C) C) Captain or above
D) D) Inspector or above
Back:
**Answer: C**

The investigating supervisor for out-of-City line of duty injuries must be in the rank of captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**3.** The desk officer must notify the Sick Desk supervisor for a LOD injury when:

A) A) The injury occurred during any part of the member's tour
B) B) The injury occurred during the first two hours of a tour
C) C) The member was treated and released from the hospital
D) D) The injury was witnessed by multiple members
Back:
**Answer: B**

Sick Desk notification is required when: incident was not specifically witnessed, injury in/near police facility, injury during first 2 hours of tour, or off-duty LOD designation request. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**4.** When a UMOS is treated at a hospital for a LOD injury, which address should be entered in hospital records?

A) A) The member's home address
B) B) The precinct of occurrence address
C) C) The Medical Division, 1 Lefrak City Plaza address
D) D) The member's permanent command address
Back:
**Answer: C**

Only the Medical Division address (1 Lefrak City Plaza, 59-17 Junction Boulevard, Corona, NY 11368) may be entered in hospital records. Member's command, home address, and telephone number will not be disclosed. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**5.** When a UMOS is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured:

A) A) No further action is required
B) B) The member may continue duty without medical evaluation
C) C) The member must be examined by a doctor for blunt body trauma or internal injury
D) D) The member is placed on sick report for 24 hours
Back:
**Answer: C**

When an injured member is struck by a bullet on a protective vest and is apparently uninjured, the member must be examined by a doctor to ascertain if a blunt body trauma or internal injury has occurred. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**6.** The Trauma Counseling follow-up meeting should occur:

A) A) 24 hours after the event
B) B) 48 hours after the event or initial contact
C) C) One week after the event
D) D) 30 days after the event
Back:
**Answer: B**

The member will be contacted for a follow-up meeting 48 hours after the event or initial contact. Additional counseling/interview sessions are scheduled within 1 to 6 weeks after the incident. (A.G. 330-07)

Front:
**7.** All conversations with the Trauma Counseling Team are:

A) A) Reported to the commanding officer
B) B) Recorded in the Command Log
C) C) Strictly confidential
D) D) Shared with Internal Affairs Bureau
Back:
**Answer: C**

ALL conversation with the Trauma Counseling Team by members of the service (uniformed and civilian) will be STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL. (A.G. 330-07)

Front:
**8.** The Trauma Counseling Team responds to all incidents involving a member being shot, killed, or seriously injured in the line of duty, AND:

A) A) Any incident at the discretion of the desk officer
B) B) Any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above
C) C) Only when directed by Internal Affairs Bureau
D) D) Only firearms discharge incidents
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Trauma Counseling Team will respond to all incidents involving MOS shot/killed/seriously injured in LOD, and any incident pursuant to a request from a captain or above. (A.G. 330-07)

Front:
**9.** The correct ratio for the bleach solution used to clean blood or body fluid spills is:

A) A) 1 part bleach to 5 parts water
B) B) 1 part bleach to 10 parts water
C) C) 1 part bleach to 20 parts water
D) D) Undiluted bleach
Back:
**Answer: B**

Accidental spills may be cleaned by applying a FRESHLY MIXED solution of 1 part household bleach to 10 parts water. The solution must be freshly mixed for each use and must not be mixed with any solution other than water. (A.G. 330-09)

Front:
**10.** When handling hypodermic needles, members should use the:

A) A) Two-handed grip technique
B) B) One-handed "scoop" technique
C) C) Pliers and container method
D) D) Gloved removal technique
Back:
**Answer: B**

Members use a one-handed "scoop" technique, using the needle itself to pick up the container, moving the needle away from themselves. Members should NOT hold the container and move their hand toward the needle. Under no circumstances should a member attempt to remove, dislodge, or bend a needle. (A.G. 330-09)

Front:
**11.** When multiple individuals are exposed to an infectious disease at a scene, who coordinates documentation?

A) A) The desk officer
B) B) The patrol supervisor at the scene
C) C) The first member to arrive
D) D) Emergency Medical Services
Back:
**Answer: B**

If there are numerous individuals involved, the patrol supervisor at the scene will be responsible for the coordination of information and documenting the names of the members of the service involved. (A.G. 330-09)

Front:
**12.** Under A.G. 330-09, to avoid unnecessary exposure at a scene, control of an aided victim suspected of having an infectious disease should be assigned to:

A) A) The patrol supervisor
B) B) EMS personnel only
C) C) An officer who has already had contact with the person
D) D) The next available officer
Back:
**Answer: C**

To avoid unnecessary exposure, control of the aided/collision victim or prisoner will be assigned to an officer at the scene who has already had contact with a person suspected of having an infectious disease. (A.G. 330-09)

Front:
**13.** If a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, the ranking officer must forward a Typed Letterhead to Medical Division within:

A) A) 30 days
B) B) 60 days
C) C) 90 days
D) D) 120 days
Back:
**Answer: B**

When a LOD designation cannot be made expeditiously, a report on Typed Letterhead indicating reasons for the delay must be forwarded to Medical Division within 60 days. Subsequent reports are forwarded for each additional 60-day period. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**14.** If an injury comes under the Federal Employees Compensation Act (FECA), the CO must forward documentation to Medical Division within:

A) A) 30 days
B) B) 60 days
C) C) 90 days
D) D) 180 days
Back:
**Answer: C**

If the injury comes under FECA, the member's CO will forward items (LOD Injury Report, witness statements, arrest history, criminal court complaint, firearms trace, completed DOL form BEC-721a) to Medical Division, Federal Compensation Liaison Officer, within 90 days of occurrence. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**15.** The bleach solution for cleaning blood spills should be disposed of:

A) A) In any available drain
B) B) In a urinal or toilet
C) C) In a sink only
D) D) In a hazardous waste container
Back:
**Answer: C**

Disposal of bleach or bleach dilutions should be performed only in a sink (not a urinal or toilet, since they sometimes contain chemical deodorizers that may cause toxic gas when mixed with bleach). (A.G. 330-09)

Front:
**16.** A UMOS going out on regular sick obtains three items from the Sick Desk. Which of the following should the UMOS provide to the desk officer at the permanent command?

A) A) Name of Sick Desk member notified
B) B) Time Sick Desk was notified
C) C) Sick serial number
D) D) Change of address/location or phone number
Back:
**Answer: C**

The UMOS receives 3 items from the Sick Desk (sick serial number, Sick Desk name, and time notified), but only provides the sick serial number to the D.O./Supervisor. (P.G. 330-01)

Front:
**17.** Administrative Sick is available to:

A) A) All members of the service
B) B) Uniformed members of the service only — CMOS cannot go Admin Sick
C) C) Civilian members of the service only
D) D) Members with more than 5 years of service
Back:
**Answer: B**

Administrative Sick is for UMOS only. Civilian members of the service cannot go Administrative Sick. (P.G. 330-02)

Front:
**18.** On the 3rd consecutive day of Administrative Sick, the member must:

A) A) Continue on Admin Sick with no changes
B) B) Report Regular Sick and be guided by Sick Desk regarding District Surgeon visit
C) C) Return to full duty
D) D) Report directly to the District Surgeon without calling anyone
Back:
**Answer: B**

Admin Sick lasts a maximum of 2 days. On the 3rd day, it converts to Regular Sick. The member is guided by the Sick Desk regarding the Medical Division visit to the District Surgeon. All three days consolidate into 1 sick event. (P.G. 330-02)

Front:
**19.** For Administrative Sick, the UMOS first calls:

A) A) The Sick Desk, then the command
B) B) The D.O./Supervisor at their command — not the Sick Desk
C) C) The District Surgeon
D) D) Operations Unit
Back:
**Answer: B**

For Admin Sick, the UMOS calls the command (D.O./Supervisor) at least 2 hours pre-tour. For Regular Sick, the UMOS calls the Sick Desk first, then the command. This is a key distinction. (P.G. 330-02)

Front:
**20.** Which of the following is NOT a reason Admin Sick would be denied (per the LIMB LSS MD mnemonic)?

A) A) Line of Duty injury
B) B) Headache
C) C) Stress/psychological
D) D) Dental problems
Back:
**Answer: B**

The LIMB LSS MD mnemonic lists: Line of duty, Injury, Mutual, Chronic B, Late (less than 2 hrs pre-tour), Stress/psychological, Supervisor deems inappropriate, Major illness, Dental. A simple headache is not on the exclusion list. (P.G. 330-02)

Front:
**21.** When a member is out sick and has a pending court appearance, the desk officer makes a Command Diary entry under:

A) A) The date of appearance only
B) B) The date of appearance and 1 day prior
C) C) The date of appearance and 2 days prior (not counting Sat/Sun/Holidays)
D) D) The date of appearance and 3 days prior
Back:
**Answer: C**

The D.O. makes a Command Diary entry under both the date of appearance AND 2 days prior, not counting Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays. (P.G. 330-04)

Front:
**22.** The Counseling Unit provides assistance to MOS experiencing difficulties with all of the following EXCEPT:

A) A) Alcohol
B) B) Prescription drugs
C) C) Gambling
D) D) Marital counseling
Back:
**Answer: D**

The Counseling Unit's primary function is assisting MOS with difficulties involving alcohol, prescription drugs, gambling, or finances. Marital counseling is not listed as a primary function. (P.G. 330-16)

Front:
**23.** A non-disciplinary referral to the Counseling Unit results in:

A) A) A report being filed with IAB
B) B) Jeopardized promotion opportunities
C) C) No report, no jeopardized promotions, and assignment not ordinarily changed
D) D) Automatic transfer to a different command
Back:
**Answer: C**

A non-disciplinary referral produces no report, does not jeopardize promotions, and the member's current assignment is not ordinarily changed (though it may be if in best interest of all). (P.G. 330-16)

Front:
**24.** The investigating supervisor for a LOD injury within the city must be:

A) A) A detective or above
B) B) A sergeant or above from the injured member's command
C) C) A captain or above
D) D) Any available supervisor regardless of command
Back:
**Answer: B**

Within the city, the investigating supervisor is a sergeant or above from the injured member's command. If unavailable, a supervisor from the precinct of occurrence. Out of city requires captain or above. (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**25.** Regarding the investigating supervisor and the three LOD reports (PAR, AIDED, LODI), which statement is correct?

A) A) IS always prepares all three reports
B) B) IS always prepares the PAR, never prepares the AIDED, and sometimes prepares the LODI
C) C) IS never prepares any of the three reports
D) D) IS always prepares the AIDED and the LODI
Back:
**Answer: B**

The Investigating Supervisor always prepares the Police Accident Report. The IS never prepares the AIDED (directs injured MOS to prepare it, or assigns another member if incapacitated). The IS sometimes prepares the LODI (only when the injured member is incapacitated). (A.G. 330-03)

Front:
**1.** How often must each uniformed and civilian member of the service be evaluated?

A) A) Semi-annually
B) B) At least once each year
C) C) Quarterly
D) D) Every eighteen months
Back:
**Answer: B**

Each uniformed and civilian member of the service must be evaluated at least once each year. (A.G. 331-01, Purpose)

Front:
**2.** When a rater evaluates a member and rates any Performance Area/Behavioral Dimension below competent, the rater must:

A) A) Immediately notify IAB
B) B) Prepare a transfer evaluation
C) C) Comment on all areas rated below competent (rated 2 or 1)
D) D) Forward the evaluation to the Chief of Department
Back:
**Answer: C**

The rater must comment on all Performance Areas/Behavioral Dimensions rated below competent (i.e., rated either 2 or 1). (A.G. 331-01, step #5)

Front:
**3.** If a ratee receives an overall evaluation of below competent (2.5 or lower) or is rated extremely competent (5.0), the reviewer must be in the rank of:

A) A) Sergeant or above
B) B) Lieutenant or above
C) C) Captain or above
D) D) Inspector or above
Back:
**Answer: C**

The reviewer must be in the rank of captain or above, or selected civilian managerial titles. (A.G. 331-01, step #24a)

Front:
**4.** A probationary police officer is evaluated via the Online Evaluation System how many times during the probationary period?

A) A) Two times (10th and 22nd month)
B) B) Three times (10th, 16th, and 22nd month)
C) C) Four times (4th, 10th, 16th, and 22nd month)
D) D) Two times (4th and 10th month)
Back:
**Answer: B**

Probationary police officers are evaluated three times during their probationary period: the tenth, sixteenth, and twenty-second month. (A.G. 331-02, step #5)

Front:
**5.** The twenty-second month evaluation of a probationary police officer must specifically recommend which of the following?

A) A) Satisfactory or Unsatisfactory
B) B) Permanent Appointment, Extension of Probation, or Termination of Services
C) C) Competent, Below Competent, or Highly Competent
D) D) Continue Probation or End Probation
Back:
**Answer: B**

The twenty-second month evaluation must specifically recommend "Permanent Appointment," "Extension of Probation" or "Termination of Services" in the reviewer's comments area. (A.G. 331-02, step #6)

Front:
**6.** Police officers assigned to investigative (Detective Track) duties are evaluated how many times during their initial fifteen months?

A) A) Once (at the fifteenth month)
B) B) Twice (eighth and fifteenth month)
C) C) Three times (fifth, tenth, and fifteenth month)
D) D) Four times (quarterly)
Back:
**Answer: B**

Police officers assigned to investigative duties are evaluated two times during their initial fifteen months — at the eighth and fifteenth month. (A.G. 331-03, step #1)

Front:
**7.** Under the PERF System for non-investigative officers, a total annual score of 77-89 corresponds to which overall evaluation rating?

A) A) Exceptional
B) B) Exceeds Expectations
C) C) Meets Standards
D) D) Needs Improvement
Back:
**Answer: B**

A total annual score of 77-89 corresponds to "Exceeds Expectations." The scale is: 90-100 Exceptional, 77-89 Exceeds Expectations, 68-76 Meets Standards, 60-67 Needs Improvement, 59 or below Unsatisfactory. (A.G. 331-04, step #10)

Front:
**8.** Probationary sergeants are evaluated how many times during their probationary period?

A) A) Once (at the tenth month)
B) B) Twice (fourth and tenth month)
C) C) Three times (fourth, tenth, and sixteenth month)
D) D) Twice (eighth and fifteenth month)
Back:
**Answer: B**

Probationary sergeants are evaluated two times during their probationary period, at the fourth and tenth month. The sixteenth month evaluation is only required if probation has been extended. (A.G. 331-05, step #1)

Front:
**9.** When evaluating a sergeant assigned as a precinct detective squad supervisor, the rater must confer with whom?

A) A) The Borough Adjutant
B) B) The precinct commanding officer
C) C) The Chief of Detectives
D) D) The Detective Bureau Commander
Back:
**Answer: B**

The rater must confer with the commanding officer when evaluating sergeants or lieutenants assigned as precinct detective squad supervisors/commanders, and record this conferral in the "Overall Rater's Comments" section. (A.G. 331-05, step #3)

Front:
**10.** Under A.G. 331-04, annual performance evaluations for police officers assigned to non-investigative Bureaus must be completed and finalized by what date?

A) A) December 31st
B) B) January 15th
C) C) January 31st
D) D) February 15th
Back:
**Answer: C**

Annual performance evaluations must be completed and finalized by January 31st of each year. (A.G. 331-04, step #28)

Front:
**11.** When a member designated "Chronic Absent - Category B" is assigned to investigative duties, it is presumed that the investigator is:

A) A) Performing satisfactorily
B) B) Eligible for promotion
C) C) Performing in a substandard manner
D) D) In need of a transfer evaluation
Back:
**Answer: C**

When an investigator is designated "Chronic Absent - Category B," it is presumed that the investigator is performing in a substandard manner. (A.G. 331-03, NOTE after step #5)

Front:
**12.** The evaluation system used for captains through deputy chiefs is based on what management concept?

A) A) Behavioral Dimensions Assessment
B) B) Quarterly Performance Review
C) C) Management By Objectives (MBO) with five Key Result Areas
D) D) Competency-Based Evaluation
Back:
**Answer: C**

The evaluation instrument for captains through deputy chiefs is based on a Management By Objectives (MBO) system with five Key Result Areas (KRAs). (A.G. 331-06, step #3 and NOTE)

Front:
**1.** A supervisor becomes aware of an allegation of employment discrimination. The supervisor should:

A) A) Investigate the complaint and prepare a report
B) B) Report to OEEO by telephone and make no Department record entries
C) C) Prepare a Command Log Entry with full details of the allegation
D) D) Refer the matter to IAB for investigation
Back:
**Answer: B**

Supervisors are mandated to report to OEEO ASAP or by next business day. They must NOT investigate and must NOT make any entries in Department records. Confidentiality is paramount. (A.G. 332-01)

Front:
**2.** Filing deadlines for employment discrimination complaints are measured from:

A) A) The date the complaint is filed
B) B) The date the original report was made
C) C) The date of occurrence
D) D) The date the respondent is notified
Back:
**Answer: C**

All filing deadlines run from the date the discriminatory act occurred, not the date it was first reported or the date the complaint is filed. (A.G. 332-01)

Front:
**3.** A sexual harassment complaint filed at the NYC level has a filing deadline of:

A) A) 300 days
B) B) 1 year
C) C) 3 years
D) D) 5 years
Back:
**Answer: C**

Sexual harassment complaints at the NYC level (NYPD or NYC Commission on Human Rights) have a 3-year filing deadline — longer than the standard 1-year deadline for other discrimination complaints. Federal is 300 days regardless. (A.G. 332-01)

Front:
**4.** A supervisor discovers offensive graffiti in the locker room. After photographing and securing the material, the supervisor should voucher it as:

A) A) Evidence
B) B) Investigatory Evidence
C) C) Found Property
D) D) Safeguarded Material
Back:
**Answer: B**

Offensive material should be safeguarded and vouchered as "Investigatory Evidence." A CLE should be made but must NOT describe or identify the specific material. (A.G. 332-02)

Front:
**5.** Which of the following is a key difference between A.G. 332-01 (Discrimination) and A.G. 332-02 (Offensive Material)?

A) A) Both require a Command Log Entry
B) B) 332-01 requires immediate investigation; 332-02 does not
C) C) 332-01 requires no Department record entries; 332-02 requires a CLE
D) D) 332-02 allows anonymous reporting; 332-01 does not
Back:
**Answer: C**

For 332-01 (Discrimination), supervisors make NO entries in any official Department record. For 332-02 (Offensive Material), a CLE is required — but it must NOT describe or identify the specific material. Additionally, for 332-01 a Report (49) is only done if a case # is received, while for 332-02 the Report (49) is automatic. (A.G. 332-01, A.G. 332-02)

Front:
**6.** A UMOS with an approved off-duty employment application wants to exceed 20 hours per week while on Family Medical Leave. This is:

A) A) Permitted with C.O. approval
B) B) Permitted without any special approval
C) C) Not permitted — the 20-hour limit applies on Family Medical Leave
D) D) Permitted only if the member obtains ERS approval
Back:
**Answer: C**

Members CANNOT exceed 20 hours per week while on Family Medical Leave. The exception to the 20-hour limit applies only to Vacation or Terminal Leave. (A.G. 332-04)

Front:
**7.** A UMOS applies for off-duty employment in a security-related field. The application should be forwarded for approval to:

A) A) The commanding officer of the command
B) B) The Borough Commanding Officer
C) C) Employee Resources Section directly
D) D) The Chief of Personnel
Back:
**Answer: B**

Security-related ODE applications are forwarded to the Boro C.O. for recommendation and signature. The command-level C.O. does not approve security jobs — the Boro C.O. does, then sends it back to the command. (A.G. 332-04)

Front:
**8.** After receiving an OEEO complaint, the respondent has the right to respond in writing within:

A) A) 7 calendar days
B) B) 10 business days
C) C) 14 calendar days
D) D) 30 calendar days
Back:
**Answer: C**

After receiving a copy of the complaint, the respondent has the right to respond in writing within 14 calendar days. If resolution is reached through mediation, NO entry is made on the respondent's CPI. (A.G. 332-01)

Front:
**9.** A member who is retaliated against for voluntarily reporting corruption should notify:

A) A) OEEO
B) B) IAB
C) C) The desk officer
D) D) The Counseling Unit
Back:
**Answer: B**

Retaliation for voluntarily providing information about misconduct or corruption is reported to IAB. If the retaliation is specifically for EEO issues, then comply with A.G. 332-01 and report to OEEO. (A.G. 332-14)

Front:
**10.** Which of the following is a Report (49) NOT required for in connection with an OEEO complaint?

A) A) When a case number is assigned
B) B) When offensive material is discovered
C) C) When the C.O. is directed by OEEO to report corrective action
D) D) When only an inquiry number is assigned
Back:
**Answer: D**

A Report (49) is only prepared when a Case # is received. If the complaint results in only an Inquiry #, no Report (49) is written. For offensive material (A.G. 332-02), the Report (49) is automatic regardless. (A.G. 332-01)

Front:
**11.** After a UMOS's off-duty employment application is approved, the application must be renewed:

A) A) Every 6 months, submitted 5 days prior to expiration
B) B) Every 12 months, submitted 10 days prior to expiration
C) C) Every 24 months, submitted 30 days prior to expiration
D) D) Only when there is a change in employment status
Back:
**Answer: B**

After approval, the ODE application must be renewed after 12 months. The renewal application must be submitted 10 days prior to expiration. Any change in status (duties, location, employer) requires a new application. (A.G. 332-04)

Front:
**12.** A Federal firearms license copy must be provided to:

A) A) C.O. only
B) B) C.O. and ERS
C) C) ERS only
D) D) C.O. and FTS
Back:
**Answer: B**

A Federal firearms license copy goes to BOTH the C.O. and ERS. A Local firearms license copy goes to the C.O. only. License numbers are also recorded on the Force Record (10 Card). (A.G. 332-04)
